## TOPIC 00036: Entering the Customer Information

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`

To delete a customer entry, begin typing the name in the Customer Name field. When a list of matching names appears, use the up/down arrow keys to h

### Entering the Customer Information

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-B905602F-39B5-4B29-B265-5CB0798A146C-a5.gif']

1. Enter the customer name and address. Calibration Executive stores this information and
 retrieves it the next time you begin to enter the customer name. 
 Note To delete a customer entry, begin typing the name in the
 Customer Name field. When a list of matching
 names appears, use the up/down arrow keys to highlight the name, as shown in
 the figure below, and press the Delete key. Click
 Delete to delete the customer entry from the
 database. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-76388F93-D86A-4792-BC41-BF00B57DDD4B-a5.gif']
2. Optional: 
 Enter information in the Work Order Number field.
3. Enter any additional relevant information in the Notes
 field.
4. Click Next.

- source_description: The following example is the uncertainty calculation for the PCI-MIO-16XE-10 at the 9.98 V analog input Test Point, 20 V range, bipolar mode (±10 V), using a Fluke 5720A calibrator that was calibrated one year ago. Calibrator UncertaintyThe specification for the Fluke 5720A calibrator at 1 year, 95%

### Example: Calculating the Uncertainty for a Test Point

The following example is the uncertainty calculation for the PCI-MIO-16XE-10 at the 9.98 V
 analog input Test Point, 20 V range, bipolar mode (±10 V), using a Fluke 5720A calibrator that
 was calibrated one year ago.

#### Calibrator Uncertainty

The specification for the Fluke 5720A calibrator at 1 year, 95% confidence level (k = 1.96 for
 normal distribution) is 3.5 ppm + 2.5 µV.

#### Noise and Other Type A Uncertainty

## TOPIC 00038: Updates and Changes for Calibration Executive Extended Support Versions

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `extended-support-changes.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/extended-support-changes.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Browse updates and changes made in Calibration Executive versions on extended support. If you cannot find changes for your version, it might be a more recent version, documented as a new feature. Or, your version might not have included user-facing updates. You can find more information about non-vi

### Updates and Changes for Calibration Executive Extended Support Versions

Browse updates and changes made in Calibration Executive versions
 on extended support.

Note

Release Notes

Related concepts:

- Calibration Executive New Features and Changes

#### Calibration Executive 6.9 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.9.

- Added driver support for Rohde & Schwarz NRP33S Power Sensor
- Added product support for NI 9262

#### Calibration Executive 6.8 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.8.

- Added support for the following products
  - PXIe-5163
  - PXIe-5698
- Removed support for the following procedures
  - PXIe-5160 (2CH)
  - PXIe-5160 (4CH)
  - PXIe-5162 (2CH)
  - PXIe-5162 (4CH)
  - PXIe-5164
  - PXIe-5650
  - PXIe-5651
  - PXIe-5652
  - PXIe-5663E
  - PXIe-5673E 3.3GHz
  - PXIe-5673E 6.6GHz
- Removed NI USB-5681 calibration procedure support
- Removed NI USB-5684 power meter standard support

#### Calibration Executive 6.7 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.7.

- Added support for the following products
  - FD-11613
  - FD-11614
  - NI 9210
  - NI TB-9212 with mini-TC
  - PXIe-4135 (40W)
  - PXIe-4137 (40W)
- Updated the following procedures
  - NI 9219
  - PXIe-4081 (90 day)
  - PXIe-4081 (2 yr)
  - PXIe-4137
  - RM-26999

#### Calibration Executive 6.6 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.6.

- Added driver support for Rohde & Schwarz SMA100B signal generator for procedures
 that previously supported the Rohde & Schwarz SMA100A
- Updated the following procedures
  - PXIe-5160
  - PXIe-5162
  - PXIe-5644R
  - PXIe-5645R
  - PXIe-5646R

#### Calibration Executive 6.5 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.5.

- Added product support for the PXIe-4139 (40W)
- Updated the following procedures
  - PXIe-4081 (90 day)
  - PXIe-4081 (2 yr)
  - PXIe-4135
  - PXIe-4139
  - PXIe-5840
  - PXIe-5841
  - NI 9212

#### Calibration Executive 6.4 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.4.

- Revised the reporting of guard band values with dB and dBv units on the calibration
 report
- Import uncertainties from an Open XML spreadsheet using formulas that result in text
 values
- Added support for the following products
  - PXIe-5841
  - PXIe-5841 (2 yr)
  - RM-26999
- Updated the guard band calculation for uncertainties with dB and dBv units
- Updated the following procedures
  - PCI-4461
  - PXI-4461
  - PXIe-5654 (10 GHz)
  - PXIe-5654 (20 GHz)
  - PXIe-5654 with PXIe-5696 (10 GHz) Adjust Only
  - PXIe-5654 with PXIe-5696 (20 GHz)
  - PXIe-5668 (14 GHz), all bandwidth options
  - PXIe-5840
  - PXIe-5840 (2 yr)
  - NI 9220
  - NI 9263

#### Calibration Executive 6.3 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.3.

- Revised formulas for determining measurement uncertainty related to TURs and nominal
 values
- Updated the TUR calculation for uncertainties with dB and dBv units
- Updated the following procedures
  - PXIe-4135
  - PXI-5422

#### Calibration Executive 6.2 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.2.

- Added support for the following products
  - PXIe-5624
  - PXIe-5606 Adjust Only
  - PXIe-5668 Adjust Only
  - USB-6346 (Screw Terminal)
  - STS-DIO-01
- Updated the following procedures
  - PXI-4110
  - PCI-5412
  - PXI-5412
  - PCI-5421
  - PXI-5421
  - PXI-5441
  - PXIe-5442
  - PXIe-5653
  - PXIe-5665 (3.6 GHz)
  - PXIe-5665 (3.6 GHz), no pre-amp
  - PXIe-5665 (14 GHz)
  - PXIe-5668 (14 GHz), all bandwidth options
  - PXIe-5668 (26.5 GHz), all bandwidth options

#### Calibration Executive 6.1 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.1.

- Added support for the following products
  - NI 9224
  - NI 9228

#### Calibration Executive 6.01 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.01.

- A critical bug in the Verify ALC Limits Table test was fixed, enabling the following
 devices to be verified and adjusted
  - PXI-5650
  - PXIe-5650
  - PXI-5651
  - PXIe-5651
  - PXI-5652
  - PXIe-5652
  - PXIe-5663
  - PXIe-5663E
  - PXIe-5673
  - PXIe-5673E

#### Calibration Executive 6.0 Changes

Learn about new features, behavior changes, and other updates in Calibration Executive
 6.0.

- Uncertainty and guard band options in custom report configurations
- Added support for the following products
  - PXIe-6349
  - USB-6349
  - NI 9202
  - NI 9252
- Added adjustment support for NI 9203
- Modified audio notifications can only be enabled or disabled; there are no additional
 options to be configured
- Removed support for 32-bit OS calibration procedures

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=fd-11601-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00039: Calibrating FD-11601 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `fd-11601-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/fd-11601-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the FD-11601 Voltage Input Device for FieldDAQ using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Adjust Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Module Warmup Verify Only Verify & Adjust Adjust Only FD-11601 10 minutes 5 minutes

### Calibrating FD-11601 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the FD-11601 Voltage Input Device for FieldDAQ using Calibration
 Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Module | Warmup | Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Adjust Only |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FD-11601 | 10 minutes | 5 minutes | 10 minutes | 5 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Calibrator | Fluke 5522A or 5560A | High-precision voltage source with gain uncertainty of ≤50 ppm and offset uncertainty of ≤50 μV |
| SHM125M-Pigtail I/O Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (x4) | Part number 785950-0R3/01/03 | — |
| Banana plug (optional) (x2) | — | Use banana plugs with an opening large enough to accommodate four 22 AWG signal wires |
| Power supply | IP67 Power Supply, part number 147464-01 or PS-16 Power Supply, part number 781094-01 | — |
| M125F-Pigtail Power Cable (1 m, 2 m, 3 m lengths) | Part number 786172-01/02/03 | — |
| M125F-M125M Power Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (optional) | Part number 786173-0R3/01/03 | Required for daisy-chaining multiple FieldDAQ devices |
| SHM128M-RJ45 Ethernet Cable (1 m, 2 m, 3 m, 10 m lengths) | Part number 785944-01/02/03/10 | — |
| SHM128M-SHM128M Ethernet Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (optional) | Part number 785946-0R3/01/03 | Required for daisy-chaining multiple FieldDAQ devices |

#### Test
 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required to ensure
 the FD-11601 meets published specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act as
 antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device are secure.
- Use shielded copper wire for all cable connections to the device. Use twisted-pairs wire
 to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ± 5 °C . The device temperature will be greater than the ambient
 temperature.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 10 minutes to ensure that the FieldDAQ device
 measurement circuitry is at a stable operating temperature.

#### Device Setup

To set up the device for
 calibration, complete the following steps:

1. Set up the FD-11601 as outlined in the FD-11601 User Manual . Note NI recommends that you use a dedicated
 Ethernet network adapter to connect the FD-11601 to a computer or controller. Use a
 second Ethernet network adapter for additional network access.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

Note

#### Test Limit
 Equations

The following test limits are derived from the published
 specifications:

TestLimits

=

TestValue

±

(

| TestValue |

×

ppm of reading

1000000

+

Range

×

ppm of range

1000000

)

Parent topic:

Calibrating FieldDAQ Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related information:

- FD-11601 User Manual

## TOPIC 00040: Calibrating FD-11613/11614 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `fd-11613-11614-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/fd-11613-11614-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the FD-11613 or the FD-11614 temperature input device for FieldDAQ using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Module Warmup Verify Only Verify & Adjust FD-11613 10 minutes 10 minutes 15

### Calibrating FD-11613/11614 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the FD-11613 or the FD-11614 temperature input device for FieldDAQ using
 Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Module | Warmup | Verify Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FD-11613 | 10 minutes | 10 minutes | 15 minutes |
| FD-11614 | 10 minutes | 15 minutes | 25 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Calibrator | Fluke 5522A locked in the 3.3 V range | A high-precision voltage source with an uncertainty of ≤70 ppm when sourcing up to 50 µA. |
| Mini Thermocouple Connector (x8) | Omega SMPW-U-M | U type For more information on the connections that are required for this procedure, see FD-11613/11614 Calibration Procedure on ni.com/docs. |
| Power supply | IP67 Power Supply, part number 147464-01 or PS-16 Power Supply, part number 781094-01 | — |
| M125F-Pigtail Power Cable (1 m, 2 m, 3 m lengths) | Part number 786172-01/02/03 | — |
| M125F-M125M Power Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (optional) | Part number 786173-0R3/01/03 | Required for daisy-chaining multiple FieldDAQ devices |
| SHM128M-RJ45 Ethernet Cable (1 m, 2 m, 3 m, 10 m lengths) | Part number 785944-01/02/03/10 | — |
| SHM128M-SHM128M Ethernet Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (optional) | Part number 785946-0R3/01/03 | Required for daisy-chaining multiple FieldDAQ devices |

#### Test
 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required to ensure
 that the FD-11613 or the FD-11614 meets published specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act as
 antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device are secure.
- Use shielded copper wire for all cable connections to the device. Use twisted-pair
 Teflon wire to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ± 5 °C . The device temperature will be greater than the ambient
 temperature.
- For valid test limits, maintain the device temperature within ± 10 °C from the last external calibration.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 10 minutes to ensure that the measurement circuitry is
 at a stable operating temperature.

#### Device Setup

To set up the device for
 calibration, complete the following steps:

1. Set up the FD-11613 or the FD-11614 as described in the FD-11613 User Guide or the
 FD-11614 User Manual . Note NI recommends that you use a dedicated Ethernet network adapter to connect the FD-11613
 or the FD-11614 to a computer or controller. Use a second Ethernet network adapter for
 additional network access.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

Note

#### Test Limit
 Equations

The following test limits are derived from the published
 specifications:

TestLimits

=

TestValue

±

(

| TestValue |

×

ppm of reading

1000000

+

Range

×

ppm of range

1000000

)

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

| SHM128M-SHM128M Ethernet Cable (0.3 m, 1 m, 3 m lengths) (optional) | Part number 785946-0R3/01/03 | Required for daisy-chaining multiple FieldDAQ devices |

 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required to ensure
 the FD-11634 meets published specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act as
 antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device are secure.
- Use shielded copper wire for all cable connections to the device. Use twisted-pairs wire
 to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ±5 °C . The
 device temperature will be greater than the ambient temperature.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 10 minutes to ensure that the FieldDAQ device
 measurement circuitry is at a stable operating temperature.

#### Device Setup

To set up the device for
 calibration, complete the following steps:

1. Set up the FD-11634 as outlined in the FD-11634 User Manual . Note NI recommends that you use a dedicated
 Ethernet network adapter to connect the FD-11634 to a computer or controller. Use a
 second Ethernet network adapter for additional network access.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=frequently-asked-questions.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00043: Frequently Asked Questions

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `frequently-asked-questions.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/frequently-asked-questions.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Q: What happens if I try to edit a report configuration in a location that is currently opened by another user on a shared network? A: When you click Config Reports, a Locked Report Configuration Location message is displayed. In this dialog box, you can select one of the following actions: Cancel

### Frequently Asked Questions

Q:  What happens if I try to edit a report configuration in a
 location that is currently opened by another user on a shared network?

A:  When you click Config Reports, a Locked
 Report Configuration Location message is displayed. In this dialog box, you can select
 one of the following actions:

- Cancel — Close the Custom Report Configurator to avoid
 corruption of configuration files. The Report Configuration
 Location remains the configured path, and you can use the available
 report formats from the Calibration Reports window.
- Change Location — Select a different Report Configuration
 Location to edit.
- Force Unlock — Unlock the current Report Configuration
 Location. This permits two or more users to edit the configurations at the same
 time; however, files may become corrupted. Choose this option only if you are sure
 that no one else is editing configurations at this location.

Q:  What happens if I try to import a configuration folder that
 has the same name as a configuration folder that already exists in my specified
 location?

A:  During import, a pop-up informs you that there is a conflict
 with the imported configuration. Do one of the following:

- Click Yes to overwrite the existing configuration with the
 configuration you are importing.
- Click No to import the new configuration with
 _Import# appended to the end of the configuration name. The
 number at the end indicates the instance of the imported configuration
 ( _Import1 , _Import2 , and so on). After import,
 you can rename the configuration (select the configuration and click
 Rename ).

Q:  Why is there a Migrate Reports button
 instead of the Config Reports button?

A:  Calibration Executive 6.0 uses a new custom reports stylesheet
 which is required to utilize guard banding features for 2.0 uncertainty formats.

Calibration Executive attempts to migrate stylesheets from your existing custom reports
 automatically, but if any of the stylesheets have been modified manually, they cannot be
 migrated without losing information. You may continue to use your reports without
 migrating, but you will be unable to use any of the new custom report functionality.

When you click Migrate, Calibration Executive prompts you to
 overwrite your modified stylesheets. If you continue, you will lose these modifications
 and will need to recreate any customizations you wish to retain. Calibration Executive
 does save a backup of your stylesheet, which you can use for reference.

Parent topic:

Custom Report Configurator

 Calibration Executive

- [Calibrating NI 5105 Using Calibration Executive](ni-5105-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXI/PXIe/PCI-5105 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5108/5172 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5172-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5108/5172 4-channel and 8-channel modules using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5110/5111/5113 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5110-5111-5113-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5110/5111/5113 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating NI 5114 Using Calibration Executive](ni-5114-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXI/PXIe/PCI-5114 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating NI 5122/5124/5142 Using Calibration Executive](ni-5122-5124-5142-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXI/PCI-5122/5124/5142 and PXIe-5122 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating USB-5132/5133 Using Calibration Executive](usb-5132-5133-calibration.html) Calibrate the USB-5132/5133 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating NI 5152/5153/5154 Using Calibration Executive](ni-5152-5153-5154-calibration.html) Calibrate the PCI/PXI-5152/5153/5154 and PCI-5153EX using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5160/5162 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5160-5162-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5160/5162 2-channel and 4-channel modules using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5163 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5163-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5163 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5164 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5164-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5164 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5170R/5171R Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5170r-5171r-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5170R/5171R modules using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5185/5186 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5185-5186-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5185/5186 modules using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5622 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5622-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5622 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating PXIe-5624 Using Calibration Executive](pxie-5624-calibration.html) Calibrate the PXIe-5624 using Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating NI 5922 Using Calibration Executive](ni-5922-calibration.html) Calibrate the NI 5922 using Calibration Executive.

Parent topic:

Calibrating Modular Instruments Using Calibration Executive

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=import-an-existing-report-configuration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00046: Import an Existing Report Configuration

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `import-an-existing-report-configuration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/import-an-existing-report-configuration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Imported configuration(s) are copied into the Report Configuration Location folder and are set to Active. In the Custom Report Configurator window, click Import. Browse to the folder that contains the report configuration you want to import. To import multiple report configurations, select the folde

### Import an Existing Report Configuration

Imported configuration(s) are copied into the Report Configuration Location folder and
 are set to Active.

1. In the Custom Report Configurator window, click Import .
2. Browse to the folder that contains the report configuration you want to import. Note To import
 multiple report configurations, select the folder
 that contains all of the report configurations
 that you want to import into the Custom Report
 Configurator.
3. Select the folder, and click Select Folder .
4. At the confirmation message, select OK .

Note

Frequently Asked Questions

Parent topic:

Custom Report Configurator

Related concepts:

- Frequently Asked Questions

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=launching-a-calibration-procedure.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00047: Launching a Calibration Procedure

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `launching-a-calibration-procedure.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/launching-a-calibration-procedure.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: This tutorial teaches you how to launch a calibration procedure in NI Calibration Executive. In this tutorial, you will launch a calibration procedure by completing the following tasks:

### Launching a Calibration Procedure

This tutorial teaches you how to launch a calibration procedure in NI Calibration
 Executive. In this tutorial, you will launch a calibration procedure by completing the
 following tasks:

1. Launching the Setup Wizard
2. Entering the Customer Information
3. Selecting the Required Standards
4. Entering Environmental Information and Selecting Options
5. Selecting a DUT
6. Run-Time Dialog Boxes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=launching-calibration-executive-logging-in.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00048: Launching Calibration Executive and Logging In

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `launching-calibration-executive-logging-in.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/launching-calibration-executive-logging-in.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: To log in to Calibration Executive, complete the following steps: Launch Calibration Executive by selecting Start Programs National Instruments Calibration Executive . In the Login dialog box, select either administrator or another user name from the User Name control. If you select a user name othe

### Launching Calibration Executive and Logging In

To log in to Calibration Executive, complete the following steps:

1. Launch Calibration Executive by selecting Start»Programs»National Instruments»Calibration Executive .
2. In the Login dialog box, select either administrator or another user name
 from the User Name control. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-BCB1E2C0-0D00-40C4-B96C-7CE098CC06B4-a5.gif']
3. If you select a user name other than administrator, enter a valid password. If you select
 administrator, no password is necessary. Note You should change the default
 administrator password after the initial login. Refer to *Modifying User
 Names or Passwords* for instructions on how to change the default
 password.
4. Click OK to launch the user interface.

Related tasks:

- Modifying User Names or Passwords

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=launching-the-setup-wizard.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00049: Launching the Setup Wizard

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `launching-the-setup-wizard.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/launching-the-setup-wizard.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `task`
- source_description: Complete the following steps to launch the setup wizard. Select the device to calibrate from the Device Types control in the upper left corner of the user interface. Devices connected to your system that have calibration procedure support are listed at the top of the drop-down list. Devices that app

### Launching the Setup Wizard

Complete the following steps to launch the setup
 wizard.

1. Select the device to calibrate from the Device Types control in the
 upper left corner of the user interface. 
 Devices connected to your system that have calibration procedure support are listed at
 the top of the drop-down list. Devices that appear in the list below the break line are
 selectable, but they reflect devices that are not automatically detected at the launch of
 Calibration Executive. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-EF7612C6-DCE0-4314-84A0-A89D4B1BFD52-a5.gif']
2. Click the Calibrate button to launch the setup wizard.

Parent topic:

Launching a Calibration Procedure

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

Related tasks:

- Entering the Customer Information

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=measurement-guard-banding-and-tur-support.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00050: Measurement Guard Banding and TUR Support

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `measurement-guard-banding-and-tur-support.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/measurement-guard-banding-and-tur-support.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: You can calculate the guard band value at runtime using different methods. You can then apply the values at runtime to the upper and lower limits. These values help you determine a reading is represented as Pass, Fail, or Conditional. The figures in this section illustrate how Calibration Executive

### Measurement Guard Banding and TUR Support

You can calculate the guard band value at runtime using different methods. You can then
 apply the values at runtime to the upper and lower limits. These values help you
 determine a reading is represented as Pass, Fail, or
 Conditional. The figures in this section illustrate how Calibration
 Executive applies guard banding to the limits.

#### Statement of Conformity

When a *Specifications* document
 contains a statement of conformity, NI uses decision rules and considers the level
 of risk to determine conformance with the agreed specification.

Binary
 Statement for Simple Acceptance Rule (w=0)

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-6A161F5B-78A4-4A76-884E-C97D97010BA4-a5.png']

Non-binary Statement with Guard Band

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-D39A3636-C241-4287-A53E-00E95961271A-a5.png']

#### Guard Band and TUR Calculations Supported by
 Calibration Executive

For all guard band methods, see the formulas below
 for the Acceptance Limits and Conditional Pass/Fail Limits calculations.

On
 the uncertainty worksheet, you can specify whether to apply guard band to only the
 upper or only the lower limit by adding the modifier "UL" or "LL" to the As-Found or
 As-Left Modifiers columns. By default, the guard band is applied to both upper and
 lower limits.

#### Built-in Guard Band and TUR Calculations

ANSI/NCSL Z540.3-2006 is the
 American national standard for the calibration of measurement and test equipment.
 The ANSI/NCSL Z540.3 standard recommends six methods to calculate and apply guard
 band to ensure a probability of false accepts (PFA) of ≤2%. ANSI/NCSL Z540.3-2006
 Method 5 and Method 6 described in the standard Appendix A are built into
 Calibration Executive.

In Calibration Executive, the lookup name
 method5 uses a formula that complies with ANSI/NCSL Z540.3-2006
 Method 5 to calculate guard bands. This method sets the guard band to be equal to
 the measurement uncertainty for each test point.

The method6
 lookup names in Calibration Executive apply to formulas that comply with Z540.3-2006
 Method 6. For method6, calculations vary depending on the
 uncertainty units.

For test points that have valid upper and lower limits, and
 uncertainty values that are > 0, the Test Uncertainty Ratio (TUR) will be
 calculated automatically and will be available on custom reports when using the 2.0
 uncertainty template. Use the "UL" and "LL" modifiers on the uncertainty worksheet
 to configure valid limits. The TUR calculation depends on the uncertainty units
 (Linear, dB, or dBv). The following table describes the Method 6 calculations and
 TUR calculations according to the uncertainty units.

| Uncertainty Unit | Lookup Name | Formulas |
| --- | --- | --- |
| dB | method6_db | Pass/Acceptance Limit Formulas: A − [ dB ] = Nom [ dB ] + 10 × log ( 1 + A − % 100 ) A + [ dB ] = Nom [ dB ] + 10 × log ( 1 + A + % 100 ) A − % = Spec − % + U − % × M A + % = Spec + % + U + % × M Fail Limit Formulas: F − [ dB ] = Nom [ dB ] + 10 × log ( 1 + Spec − % − U − % × M 100 ) F + [ dB ] = Nom [ dB ] + 10 × log ( 1 + Spec + % − U + % × M 100 ) M = 1.04 − e ( 0.38 ln ( TUR ) − 0.54 ) Spec + % = ( 10 ( UL [ dB ] − Nom [ dB ] 10 ) − 1 ) × 100 Spec − % = ( 10 ( LL [ dB ] − Nom [ dB ] 10 ) − 1 ) × 100 TUR = 10 ( UL [ dB ] − Nom [ dB ] ) 10 − 10 ( LL [ dB ] − Nom [ dB ] ) 10 0.02 × U % U % = ( 10 ( U [ dB ] 5 ) − 1 ) × 100 ( 10 ( U [ dB ] 5 ) + 1 ) |
| dBv | method6_dbv | Pass/Acceptance Limit Formulas: A − [ dBv ] = Nom [ dBv ] + 20 × log ( 1 + A − % 100 ) A + [ dBv ] = Nom [ dBv ] + 20 × log ( 1 + A + % 100 ) A − % = Spec − % + U − % × M A + % = Spec + % + U + % × M Fail Limit Formulas: F − [ dBv ] = Nom [ dBv ] + 20 × log ( 1 + Spec − % − U − % × M 100 ) F + [ dBv ] = Nom [ dBv ] + 20 × log ( 1 + Spec + % − U + % × M 100 ) M = 1.04 − e ( 0.38 ln ( TUR ) − 0.54 ) Spec + % = ( 10 ( UL [ dBv ] − Nom [ dBv ] 20 ) − 1 ) × 100 Spec − % = ( 10 ( LL [ dBv ] − Nom [ dBv ] 20 ) − 1 ) × 100 TUR = 10 ( UL [ dBv ] − Nom [ dBv ] ) 20 − 10 ( LL [ dB ] − Nom [ dB ] ) 20 0.02 × U % U % = ( 10 ( U [ dBv ] 10 ) − 1 ) × 100 ( 10 ( U [ dBv ] 10 ) + 1 ) |
| Units other than dB and dBv | method6_linear | Guardband = U × ( 1.04 − e ( 0.38 ln ( TUR ) − 0.54 ) ) TUR = UL − LL 2 × U A − = LL ⁢ + Guardband A + = UL ⁢ + Guardband F − = LL ⁢ + Guardband F + = UL ⁢ + Guardband |
| A- = Lower Pass/Acceptance Limit A+ = Upper Pass/Acceptance Limit Nom = Nominal Value F- = Lower Fail Limit F+ = Upper Fail Limit Spec- = Lower Specification Limit Spec+ = Upper Specification Limit U- = Lower Uncertainty U+ = Upper Uncertainty LL = Lower Limit UL = Upper Limit TUR = Test Uncertainty Ratio |  |  |

When using the value method6, the uncertainty worksheet
 automatically selects the correct formula based on the uncertainty units. The method
 6 formulas in the table are only applied when both the upper limit and lower limit
 are valid, and when the calculated Test Uncertainty Ratio (TUR) is determined to be
 ≤ 4. For values greater that 4, the calculated guard band is determined to be
 0.

If a nominal value (Nom) is not specified, Calibration
 Executive uses a default nominal value:

Nom

=

(

UL

+

LL

)

2

#### Custom Guard Band Calculations

In
 addition to the built-in guard bands, you can add user-defined custom guard bands,
 which modify the limits by a fixed value that is specified for each test
 point.

When a custom guard band is added, the prefix Custom_
 is added to the user-provided tag name to form the Lookup ID for the custom guard
 band. For example, if you insert a new custom guard band and enter the tag name
 NewCalc, the Lookup ID for the guard band becomes
 Custom_NewCalc.

In this way, you can define a custom
 description for the guard band to display on the report. You must configure the
 guard band notes from within the Calibration Executive application.

#### Conditional Pass Runtime and Reporting
 Behavior

Many runtime and reporting behaviors in Calibration Executive are
 evaluated as simply "Passed" or "Failed" as long as the result falls within the
 specified range. However, you can configure runtime options to specify whether a
 conditional pass should evaluate to "Passed" or "Failed" for these specific
 behaviors.

To configure the execution and reporting behavior for a Conditional
 Pass:

1. From the toolbar in the main Calibration Executive window, select Configure»Runtime Options . [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-B21F0D75-1D16-46EE-8ACE-D6CDD4B07220-a5.png']
2. Select an option to specify whether a Conditional Pass is a Runtime Pass or a
 Runtime Fail.

By default, Calibration Executive evaluates a conditional pass as a runtime
 fail. This is equivalent to using the Accept Limits that are calculated from the
 guard band to dictate the passed failed behavior.

The following tables show
 the result type for specific behaviors when guard banding is taking into
 consideration. A Binary type means that the result will be either "Passed" or
 "Failed." Conditional means that a double dagger symbol (‡) will be appended to the
 "Passed" or "Failed" result.

| Behavior | Result Type (Binary or Conditional) |
| --- | --- |
| Failed Test Point Dialog Display Condition | Binary |
| Step message log pass/fail status | Conditional |
| Test step pass/fail result in execution dialog | Binary |
| Overall test result dialog | Binary |

| Behavior | Result Type (Binary or Conditional) |
| --- | --- |
| Overall As-Found Result | Conditional |
| Overall As-Left Result | Binary |
| Individual Test Point Result (as-found and as-left) | Conditional |

Parent topic:

Measurement Uncertainty

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=measurement-uncertainty.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00051: Measurement Uncertainty

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `measurement-uncertainty.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/measurement-uncertainty.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Measurement uncertainty is an estimated range of values around the measured value. The accepted value likely lies within this estimated range. A total uncertainty value represents a measure of the random error and the estimated bounds of the systematic error combined. This value also considers all f

### Measurement Uncertainty

Measurement uncertainty is an estimated range of values around the measured value. The accepted
 value likely lies within this estimated range. A total uncertainty value represents a
 measure of the random error and the estimated bounds of the systematic error combined.
 This value also considers all factors that contribute to the measurement uncertainty.
 For detailed information about measurement uncertainty, refer to *ANSI/NCSL
 Z540-2-1997, U.S. Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement*.

Calibration Executive provides tools that allow laboratories to manage the uncertainties for each
 test point of a procedure based on their lab capabilities. These tools also allow users
 to capture uncertainties in reports.

You can also use uncertainties to calculate guard banding. You can apply guard banding to each
 test point at runtime and in the report. For more information on guard banding, see
 *Measurement Guard Banding and TUR Support*.

#### Displaying
 Measurement Uncertainty in Calibration Executive Reports

In Calibration
 Executive, use the Uncertainty Management utility to show
 uncertainties associated with procedures. You can also use this utility to include
 uncertainties and guard banding in reports. Users with Administrator privileges can
 access this utility from the Calibration Reports window.

Complete the
 following steps to launch the Uncertainty Management utility.

1. Select View»Calibration Reports .
2. On the Calibration Reports window, click Manage
 Uncertainty .

Use the Uncertainty Export/Import window to export a template. You can use this
 template to edit uncertainties and guard bands for each test point in the procedure.
 Import the edited values to an uncertainties database that appends the uncertainties
 to future runs of the procedure.

#### Exporting
 and Editing Uncertainty Templates

Export an uncertainty template from an
 existing report for a procedure that has completed without terminating. You cannot
 export an uncertainty template if a procedure does not have a completed run with a
 report.

The following types of uncertainty templates exist:

Legacy

2.0

Complete the following steps to export an uncertainty template.

1. In the Calibration Reports window, click Manage
 Uncertainty .
2. From the Uncertainty List in the Uncertainty
 Export/Import window, select the device you want to use for export.
3. Select Operate»Export to File or right-click the device name and select Export
 Template .
4. In the Select Export Folder dialog box, select the
 location to save the exported file. Then use the File Type to
 Export drop-down to specify the file type. Calibration Executive
 supports the following template formats:
5. .xlsx (Single Workbook) —This format (Office Open XML Spreadsheet) is compatible with many
 spreadsheet editors. For example, Microsoft Excel 2007 SP3 or later,
 Apache OpenOffice, and Google Sheets. Note For procedures
 with more than 1000 test points, Calibration Executive exports
 uncertainties to a CSV file. Refer to the XLSX file for
 instructions to copy the data manually into the XLSX file. The
 Open XML parser that Calibration Executive uses is limited to
 procedures with fewer than 1000 test points.
6. .csv (comma-delimited) —This format generates a single CSV file with all test points listed
 as comma-separated values. You can edit the file with different text
 editors and spreadsheet editors.
7. .txt (tab-delimited) —This format generates a single TXT file with all test points listed
 as tab-separated values. You can edit the file with different text
 editors and spreadsheet editors.
8. .xlsx (Legacy Workbook / Device) —Depracted format. Calibration Executive versions that are earlier than 6.0 use this
 format. You cannot export 2.0 version uncertainty templates in this
 format.
9. Click Done to return to the Calibration Reports
 window.

1. Locate the folder where you saved the uncertainty template. The default path
 begins with C:\Program Files\National Instruments\Calibration
 Executive\Uncertainty\ .
2. Open the template and edit the appropriate fields to add uncertainties.
3. Save the updated uncertainty template.

#### Legacy and
 2.0 Fields

The following uncertainty data fields from the legacy and 2.0
 templates are editable:

Uncertainty Version

1.0

Uncertainty Version

Uncertainty (dbl)

0

For
 example, if the uncertainty value for a test point is 0.0012
 V, the report displays the uncertainty value as
 1.2E-3 V. The readings and limits show four digits
 of precision, for example, 1.1234 V.

Uncertainty Units

As Found Uncertainty Notes

As Left Uncertainty Notes

#### 2.0-Only
 Fields

As Found/As Left Guardband Type

- none
- method 5
- method 6
- method 6 linear
- method 6 db
- method 6 dbv
- custom_ <name>

As Found/As Left Modifiers

Measurement Guard Banding

customval=1.23E-2;UL

- customval = <value> , where
 <value> is a floating point numeric
 (for example, 1.234 ) or an exponential (for
 example, 1.23E-6 )
- UL (only the upper limit is valid)
- LL (only the lower limit is valid)
- nominal = <value> is a floating point
 numeric (for example, 1.234 ) or an exponential
 (for example, 1.23E-6 )
- nominal=1.0E-1 —If you do not specify units, the units are assumed
 to be equivalent to the limits unit without an SI
 prefix. That is, if the limits unit column contains
 mV , the nominal value is assumed
 to be 1.0E-1 V . This magnitude is
 applied to the TUR formulas.
- nominal=1.0E-1 mV —If you specify units as in this example, you can
 also specify an SI prefix. Specifying a prefix makes
 it easier to compare the nominal value to the limits
 or values with the same prefix. At runtime, the
 value is converted to the specified prefix to match
 the magnitude of the limits unit.

CMC (dbl)

1 mV

0.5 mV

5E-4

Note

Uncertainty

Uncertainty Units

Notes

#### Importing
 Uncertainties from a Template File

After you edit the template file,
 import the values to the uncertainties database. Calibration Executive imports only
 the values in the uncertainty data fields. Do not modify the other fields, as
 Calibration Executive uses many of those data fields as reference.

You can
 import uncertainties from an Open XML spreadsheet and use a formula to calculate the
 uncertainty. In this case, only the value that the formula calculates is imported
 into the database. Calibration Executive supports only formulas whose results are
 numeric or text values.

1. From the Calibration Reports window, click
 Manage Uncertainty .
2. In the Uncertainty Export/Import window, select Operate»Import from File .
3. Browse to the location of the uncertainty template that you want to
 import.
4. Select the template and click OK .
5. In the Uncertainty Export/Import window, click
 Done .

Once you import the uncertainties to the database, the template file is
 no longer needed. However, the file might be useful later for tracking
 purposes.

#### Updating
 Guard Band Notes

Use guard band notes to describe the guard band method in
 the calibration certificate. Calibration Executive adds the notes to the calibration
 certificate when you run the procedure. You can edit the descriptions of guard band
 notes and report texts for both built-in and custom guard band methods.

To
 view the Guardband Notes table, select Operate»Edit Guardband Notes from the menu bar on the Uncertainty
 Export/Import window.

To edit a
 Description or Report Text field,
 right-click the text. Then select Edit Guardband Description
 or Edit Guardband Notes.

To add a custom guard band,
 right-click a line of text and select Insert New Custom
 Guardband. After you enter the tag name, you can edit the
 description and the report text.

To delete a custom guard band, right-click
 the guard band and select Delete Custom Guardband. You cannot
 delete built-in guard band methods.

#### Updating
 Uncertainty Version and Notes

Update uncertainty versions and uncertainty
 notes directly from the Uncertainty Export/Import window.
 Right-click the version or note that you want to update and follow the instructions.
 These fields are also available in the exported template. You can modify the fields
 in the template before you import the template.

#### Multiple
 Uncertainties

You can import multiple versions of uncertainties for each
 procedure. This way, you can use different values under different circumstances. You
 can import additional uncertainty versions after you import the first version of the
 uncertainties. You must add a unique version to the template worksheet each time you
 import the file. Calibration Executive adds the version values to the database. Then
 the new version appears in the list of uncertainties on the Uncertainty
 Export/Import window.

#### Adding
 Uncertainties to a Report

After you add the uncertainties for a procedure,
 use the Options and Environmental Information page in the
 setup wizard. Here, you can select which version of the uncertainties you want to
 display on the report. The Uncertainty Version selection box
 lists all active versions, along with the option of not reporting the
 uncertainty.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-40765031-088A-40DF-BC93-E502F3F22432-a5.png']

If your station does not
 require uncertainties, you can disable uncertainty version control. Go to Configure»Report Options and set the global uncertainties option to <No
 Uncertainty>.

#### Modifying
 Uncertainties for a Procedure

If you must modify the uncertainties for a
 procedure, change and re-import the previous template. You can also create a
 template that is preloaded with the current uncertainties. For older uncertainties,
 you can only create a template if a valid report exists on the station. This
 limitation also exists when creating the original template. You do not need a report
 to export 2.0 uncertainties. To export a 2.0 format uncertainty, select Operate»Export to File. You can also right-click the uncertainty and select
 Export.

#### Deleting
 Uncertainties

To delete uncertainties, select Operate»Delete Items. Calibration Executive displays all uncertainties stored in the
 database. Select the uncertainties that you want to delete, then click
 Delete. You can also right-click an uncertainty that you
 want to delete and select Delete Uncertainty from
 DB.

Notice

#### Updating
 Uncertainties on Existing Reports

NI recommends that you add uncertainties
 by selecting the appropriate uncertainty before running a procedure. However, you
 can generate a report with a different set of uncertainties from the Calibration
 Reports window.

In the Calibration Reports window, use the
 Uncertainty Version option to select the uncertainty
 version that the report displays. The default version is the version with which the
 report was originally generated.

Notice

- For the readings and the limits, Calibration Executive does not change the
 digits of precision from the original report. This behavior might result in
 an undesired discrepancy.
- You cannot display guard band information on your report. Calibration
 Executive determines guard band details at runtime only. Calibration
 Executive does not recalculate the guard band details later.

#### Importing
 Uncertainties for New Procedure Versions

When upgrading Calibration
 Executive, procedure versions might change if the procedure is modified. If the
 procedure version changes, the uncertainties for the previous versions of the
 procedure are not compatible with the new versions. In this case, you must import a
 new version of the uncertainties. To import a new version, you must generate a new
 report for each new version of a procedure. Generating a new report ensures that
 Calibration Executive creates an accurate template.

If you must add
 uncertainties for multiple versions of a procedure, you must create and import a
 separate template for each version.

You can determine the procedure version of
 your stored uncertainty through the Uncertainty
 Management utility. To check the current procedure
 version, select the device from the Device Types control.
 Then select Help»About Procedure. The About Procedure dialog box displays the
 procedure name, the version of the procedure, and a list of changes.

#### Uncertainty
 Database Options

Use the Uncertainty Management
 Export/Import window to specify or change the location of the
 uncertainty database. You can set the location to a networked location that multiple
 systems can share.

You can also import uncertainties that are created on other
 systems into your local system by using the menu options in the
 Uncertainty Management Export/Import
 window.

Parent topic:

Viewing a Calibration Report

Related concepts:

- Measurement Guard Banding and TUR Support
- Custom Report Configurator
- Example: Calculating the Uncertainty for a Test Point

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=menu-bar.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00052: Menu Bar

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `menu-bar.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/menu-bar.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: The Calibration Executive menu bar contains the following four menus. FileThe File menu contains the following options: Logout—Logs out the current user and enables the Login selection under the File menu. Exit—Closes Calibration Executive. If a calibration procedure is currently running, closing Ca

### Menu Bar

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-F616C944-6CCE-42E9-95AC-6781894C7EC4-a5.gif']

#### File

The File
 menu contains the following options:

- Logout —Logs out the
 current user and enables the Login selection under
 the File menu.
- Exit —Closes
 Calibration Executive. Note If
 a calibration procedure is currently running,
 closing Calibration Executive may cause errors.
 Make sure you click the
 Terminate button before you
 exit Calibration Executive.
- Login —Allows a new user
 to log in to the application.

#### View

The view
 menu contains the following option.

- Calibration
 Reports —Opens the Calibration Reports
 window. Use this window to view or delete a stored
 calibration report.

#### Configure

The
 Configure menu contains the following
 options.

- Audio
 Notifications —Enables or disables
 audio notifications in Calibration Executive.
- Report Options —Configures
 how Calibration Executive displays calibration
 reports.
- Database
 Options —Determines the database that
 stores calibration reports.
- Report Database Backup
 Copy —Saves a secondary copy of the
 current calibration reports database.
- Users —Allows you to add
 new users, edit current user profiles, or delete
 users.

#### Help

The Help
 menu contains the following options:

- Calibration Executive
 Help —Launches this help file.
- Calibration Procedure
 Help— Provides procedure help for the
 device type selected.
- About
 Procedure— Provides specific
 information about the procedure used to calibrate
 the device selected.
- About Calibration
 Executive —Displays the version of
 Calibration Executive you are using.

Parent topic:

Calibration Executive User Interface

Related concepts:

- Viewing a Calibration Report

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=merge-reports.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00053: Merge Reports

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `merge-reports.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/merge-reports.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Select View Calibration Reports to display the Calibration Reports window. Highlight the two reports you want to merge. If the two reports appear to be compatible, the Merge Report button enables as shown in the following figure. The Merge Report button enabling is only a pre-checking function and d

### Merge Reports

1. Select View»Calibration Reports to display the Calibration
 Reports window.
2. Highlight the two reports you want to merge. If the two reports appear to be compatible, the
 Merge Report button enables as shown
 in the following figure. Note The
 Merge Report button enabling
 is only a pre-checking function and does not mean that two
 reports can be merged.
3. Select the report format using the Report Type pull-down menu.
4. Click Merge Reports to merge the two highlighted
 reports.
5. Edit the DUT notes if you enabled Allow Editing of the DUT notes when reports are
 merged on the Configuring Report
 Options window.
6. If the two reports are compatible, the merged report appears at the top of the report list, as
 shown in the following figure. If the reports are not compatible an
 error appears.

Parent topic:

Calibration Reports

Related concepts:

- Configuring Report Options

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=modifying-user-names-passwords.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00054: Modifying User Names or Passwords

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `modifying-user-names-passwords.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/modifying-user-names-passwords.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `task`
- source_description: Change the user name, comments, or password associated with a specific user. Navigate to Configure Users to open the Configure Users window. Highlight the name of the user you want to modify. Click Edit. Modify the information you want to change. The Advanced button displays the Edit Flags dialog bo

### Modifying User Names or Passwords

Change the user name, comments, or password associated with a specific user.

1. Navigate to Configure»Users to open the Configure Users window.
2. Highlight the name of the user you want to modify.
3. Click Edit.
4. Modify the information you want to change. 
 Note The
 Advanced button displays the Edit
 Flags dialog box, which contains TestStand property flags you
 can modify. You need to configure these property flags only when you are
 developing a relatively sophisticated custom type in TestStand. The
 Advanced button has no function in Calibration
 Executive.
5. Click OK to save your changes.
6. Click Close to close the Configure
 Users window.

Parent topic:

Launching Calibration Executive and Logging In

## TOPIC 00055: Calibrating Modular Instruments Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `modular-instruments.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/modular-instruments.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate your Modular Instruments with Calibration Executive.

### Calibrating Modular Instruments Using
 Calibration Executive

Calibrate your Modular Instruments with Calibration Executive.

- [Calibrating Digital Multimeters (DMMs) Using Calibration Executive](digital-multimeters-dmms.html) Calibrate your DMMs with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating High-Speed Digital I/O Devices Using Calibration Executive](high-speed-digital-io.html) Calibrate your high-speed digital I/O devices with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating High-Speed Digitizers Using Calibration Executive](high-speed-digitizers.html) Calibrate your high-speed digitizers with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating Power Measurement Conditioners Using Calibration Executive](power-measurement-conditioners.html) Calibrate your power measurement conditioners with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating Power Supplies and SMUs Using Calibration Executive](power-supplies-and-smus.html) Calibrate your power supplies and SMUs with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating Electronic Loads Using Calibration Executive](eloads.html) Calibrate your electronic loads with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating RF Devices Using Calibration Executive](rf-devices.html) Calibrate your RF devices with Calibration Executive.
- [Calibrating Signal Generators Using Calibration Executive](signal-generators.html) Calibrate your signal generators with Calibration Executive.
- [Testing Switches Using Calibration Executive](switches.html) Test the performance of your switches with Calibration Executive.

Parent topic:

Calibrating Devices Using Calibration Executive

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=ni-2503-2529-switch-performance-test.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00057: NI 2503/2529 Switch Performance Test

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `ni-2503-2529-switch-performance-test.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/ni-2503-2529-switch-performance-test.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Test the performance of the PXI-2503 and PXI/PXIe-2529 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Device Performance Test PXI-2503 3 minutes PXI/PXIe-2529 8 minutes Test Equipment 332 Equipment f

### NI 2503/2529 Switch Performance Test

Test the performance of the PXI-2503 and PXI/PXIe-2529 using Calibration
 Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure
 Features

| Verify only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate
 Test Time

| Device | Performance Test |
| --- | --- |
| PXI-2503 | 3 minutes |
| PXI/PXIe-2529 | 8 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| DMM | PXI-4071 | Voltage Accuracy: 7.5 digits Resistance Accuracy: 6.5 digits |
| Terminal Block | PXI-2503: TB-2606 PXI/PXIe-2529: TB-2636 | — |

The switch performance tests should be executed periodically on switches used
 with Calibration Executive procedures to check if any switch paths have become
 damaged, or if the switches are approaching the end of their useful life. The
 frequency of execution of these tests will depend on many factors, including how
 often the switches are used.

#### Test
 Conditions

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires
 act as antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device are secure.
- Use shielded copper wire for all cable connections to the device. Use
 twisted-pair wire to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ±5
 °C . The device temperature is greater than the ambient
 temperature.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.

#### Device Setup

1. Configure the hardware using Measurement &
 Automation Explorer (MAX).
2. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and
 complete the setup wizard.
3. Complete the following steps to create
 fixtures that will be used to connect the switches
 to the DMM.

#### Create the
 PXI/PXIe-2529 Fixture

1. On the TB-2636, connect the positive (+) and negative (-) terminals together for
 C0—C31.
2. Connect all + terminals together for R0 to R3, and wire them out using two
 separate wires to banana connectors labeled "DMM HI" and "DMM HI Sense".
3. Connect all - terminals together for R0 to R3, and wire them out using two
 separate wires to banana connectors labeled "DMM LO" and "DMM LO Sense".

To run the procedure, connect the TB-2636 to the PXI/PXIe-2529 switch, and
 connect the four banana connectors to the appropriate terminals on the
 DMM.

#### Create the
 PXI-2503 Fixture

1. On the TB-2606, connect the + and - terminals together for C0 to C5.
2. Connect all + terminals together for R0 to R3, and wire them out using two
 separate wires to banana connectors labeled "DMM HI" and "DMM HI Sense."
3. Connect all - terminals together for R0 to R3, and wire them out using two
 separate wires to banana connectors labeled "DMM LO" and "DMM LO Sense".

To run the procedure, connect the TB-2606 to the PXI-2503 switch, and connect
 the four banana connectors to the appropriate terminals on the DMM.

#### Test Limit
 Equations

These switch tests are not
 intended to be a validation of the full warranted
 performance of these devices. The parameters and
 limits used in these tests are based on the needs
 of the procedures in Calibration Executive that
 use these switches to ensure they meet the
 performance requirements of those
 procedures.

Parent topic:

Testing Switches Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=ni-4051-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00058: Calibrating PXIe-4051 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `ni-4051-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/ni-4051-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-4051 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 262 PXIe-4051 Test Times Run Options Warmup As-Found Verify Steps Adjust Steps As-Left Verify Steps Total Verify Only 30 minutes

### Calibrating PXIe-4051 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-4051 using Calibration
 Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate
 Test Time

| Run Options | Warmup | As-Found Verify Steps | Adjust Steps | As-Left Verify Steps | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | 30 minutes | 35 minutes | — | — | 65 minutes |
| Adjust Only | 30 minutes | — | 20 minutes | — | 50 minutes |
| Verify and Adjust | 30 minutes | 35 minutes | 20 minutes | 35 minutes | 120 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

The following table lists the test equipment required to
 calibrate the PXIe-4051.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Functional Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| DMM | Keysight 3458A | Voltage verification Current verification Current adjustment | Use a DMM capable of : Current measurements up to 1 A Voltage measurements up to 100 V Refer to the Keysight 3458A Multimeter User's Guide for information on minimum accuracy requirements. |
| Power supply | Elektro-Automatik EA-PS 9080-50 1U | Voltage verification Current verification Current adjustment | Use a power supply capable of: Outputs of up to 61 VDC and 50 A Refer to the Elektro-Automatik PS 9000 1U Operating Guide for information on minimum accuracy requirements. |
| PXIe chassis | PXIe-1095 or other high-powered chassis | All procedures | Use a high-powered chassis for this calibration procedure |
| Calibrator | Fluke 5730A | Voltage adjustment | Voltage sourcing |
| Current Shunt[1]1 Test points should be included on the accredited calibration report for CS-50: 0.35 A, 1 A, 3 A, 5 A, 10 A, 20 A, 30 A, 40 A, and 50 A. | Ohm Labs CS-50 | Current verification Current adjustment | Use a CS-50 precision shunt for this calibration procedure |
| PXIe-4051 Calibration Cable Kit | NI 789107-01Kit includes: Cal cable - "Spade", quantity 1 Cal cable - "Banana", quantity 1 Cal connector - "Shorting", quantity 1 Spade jumper, quantity 2 Red Spade-Banana jumper, quantity 2 Black Spade-Banana jumper, quantity 2 | All procedures | — |

#### Test
 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required
 to ensure the PXIe-4051 meets published
 specifications.

- For maximum accuracy, perform the autocalibration (ACAL) function of the digital
 multimeter (DMM) prior to calibration if it has not been performed within the
 last 24 hours or when the multimeter’s temperature changes by ± 1 °C from the last autocalibration. Always
 disconnect any input signals before performing autocalibration. Perform ACAL for
 all functions.
- Keep cabling as short as possible. Long cables act as antennas, picking up extra
 noise that can affect measurements.
- Ensure that all connections to the DUT, including front panel connections and
 screws, are secure.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes for all components of the
 calibration system.
- Make all connections as shown in diagrams.
- Ensure the fan filters are clean and that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more
 information, refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users 
 document available at ni.com/docs .
- If a DUT fails reverification after adjustment, ensure that the Test Conditions
 have been met before returning the DUT to NI.
- Clean any oxidation from the spade connectors or banana plugs on the cables
 before connecting them into the binding posts. Oxidation tarnishes the copper
 plugs so that they appear dull rather than shiny and leads to greater thermal
 EMF.

#### Device
 Setup

Hazardous Voltage

- Safeguard exposed terminals.
- Ensure equipment is de-energized prior to making or disconnecting
 connections.
- Use Hazardous Area signage as applicable.
- Ensure easy access to power shut-off switches.

Tension dangereuse

- Protégez les terminaux exposés.
- Assurez-vous que l'équipement est hors tension avant d'établir ou de
 déconnecter les connexions.
- Utilisez la signalisation des zones dangereuses, le cas échéant.
- Assurez-vous d'un accès facile aux interrupteurs de coupure de courant.

EA PS9080-50 1U

1. If you are setting your EA PS9080-50 1U up
 for TCP/IP communication, follow the following steps:
  1. Set up the EA PS9080-50 1U by
 following the guidance in the Initial Network Setup 
 section of the PS 9000 1U Operating Guide .
  2. Configure a new network device in NI MAX using the newly configured
 IP address and port for your EA
 PS9080-50 to interface with the power supply.
2. If you are setting your EA PS9080-50 1U up
 for USB communication, install the EA USB Driver with PS900 Support to
 enable support for USB communication with the power supply.
3. Disable Interface Monitoring on your device.
  1. Push the Menu button.
  2. Use the arrow keys to highlight
 Communications and press
 Enter .
  3. Use the arrow keys to highlight Com Timeout 
 and press Enter .
  4. Use the arrow keys to select Enable interf.
 monitoring .
  5. Use the right dial to select No and press
 Enter .
  6. Press Escape until your power supply returns
 to the voltage/current display.

To set up the PXIe-4051 for calibration,
 complete the following steps:

1. Install the PXIe-4051 according to the
 instructions in the PXIe-4051 User Manual .
2. Configure the remaining hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer
 (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

Calibration Executive guides you through the connections required for
 calibration.

#### Test
 Measured Value Equations

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| Voltage Programming and Measurement Accuracy/Resolution Verification | T e s t M e a s u r e d V a l u e = D M M M e a s u r e m e n t - D U T M e a s u r e m e n t |
| Current Programming and Measurement Accuracy/Resolution Verification | T e s t M e a s u r e d V a l u e = D M M M e a s u r e m e n t S h u n t N o m i n a l R e s i s tan c e - D U T M e a s u r e m e n t |
| Current Adjustment Measured Current | T e s t M e a s u r e d V a l u e = D M M M e a s u r e m e n t S h u n t N o m i n a l R e s i s tan c e |

Parent topic:

Calibrating Electronic Loads Using Calibration Executive

- Calibration Executive Operating Requirements

Related information:

- PXIe-4051 User Manual
- Keysight
- Elektro Automatik

[<sup>1</sup>](#note_ref-d7146e355) Test points should be included on the accredited
 calibration report for CS-50: 0.35 A, 1 A, 3 A, 5 A, 10
 A, 20 A, 30 A, 40 A, and 50
 A.

## TOPIC 00059: Calibrating NI 4065 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `ni-4065-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/ni-4065-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 75 minutesFor a list of the TestLimits used in this calibration procedure, refer to the NI 4065 6–½ Digit DMM Calibr

### Calibrating NI 4065 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

Note

ni.com/docs

ni.com/calibration

#### Test
 Equipment

The following table lists the test equipment required to
 calibrate the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Calibrator | Fluke 5700A/5720A/5730A* | You must use a Fluke 5700A/5720A/5730A to calibrate NI 4065 modules. Do not use a Fluke 5500A/5520A/5522A calibrator. The Fluke 5700A multifunction calibrator must be within the 90 day calibration interval. The Fluke 5720A/5730A multifunction calibrator must be within the one year calibration interval. |
| Cables | Fluke 5440 low thermal electromotive force (EMF) copper cables | Use two sets of low thermal electromotive force (EMF) copper cables. |
| Shorting Bar | Pomona 5145 insulated double banana plug shorting bar | Use to create a short with low thermal EMF (≤150 nV) across the HI and LO input banana plug connectors on the NI 4065. |
| Chassis | (NI PCI/PCIe-4065) A personal computer (PC) with an available PCI slot or an available x1, x4, x8, or x16 PCI Express slot or (PXI-4065) NI PXI chassis such as the PXI-1042 or the PXI-1042Q and a PXI controller | — |

Note

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and
 environmental conditions are required to ensure the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 meets
 published specifications.

- (PXI) Ensure that the PXI chassis fan speed is set to HIGH and the fan filters
 are clean.
- Use PXI/PCI filler panels in all vacant slots to allow proper cooling.
- Plug the PXI chassis or PC and the calibrator into the same power strip to avoid
 ground loops.
- Power on and warm up the calibrator for at least 60 minutes before beginning
 this calibration procedure, and power on and warm up the NI 4065 for at least 30
 minutes before beginning this calibration procedure.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 ±1 °C.
- Maintain an ambient relative humidity of less than 60%.
- Keep a shorting bar connected between the V GUARD and GROUND binding posts of
 the calibrator at all times.
- Clean any oxidation from the banana plugs on the Fluke 5440 cables before
 plugging them into the binding posts of the calibrator or the banana plug
 connectors of the NI 4065. Oxidation tarnishes the copper banana plugs so they
 appear dull rather than shiny and leads to greater thermal EMF.
- Keep the blue banana plugs on the Fluke 5440 cables connected to the V GUARD
 binding post of the calibrator at all times.
- Prevent the cables from moving or vibrating by taping or strapping them to a
 nonvibrating surface. Movement or vibration causes triboelectric effects that
 can result in measurement errors.

#### Device
 Setup

Hazardous Voltage

To set up the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 for calibration, complete the
 following steps:

1. Install the NI 4065 according to the instructions in the NI Digital Multimeters
 Getting Started Guide.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

Calibration Executive guides you through the connections required for
 calibration.

Note

Troubleshooting Guidelines

#### Troubleshooting Guidelines

#### Error Code
 –300300

NI-DMM version 2.7.0 is not compatible with Calibration Executive.
 You must install NI-DMM 2.7.1 or later to run Calibration Executive.

#### Error Code
 –18002

You will receive the following error message if NI-DMM 2.5.1 or
 earlier is installed.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-4181278E-A0C3-400C-96CA-2A4A533E2415-a5.jpg']

You must install NI-DMM 2.7.1 or later.

#### Other Issues

If the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065
 is not recognized in MAX, make sure you followed the configuration guidelines. If
 MAX fails to recognize the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 after you reconfigure, contact NI
 technical support.

If the PXI/PCI/PCIe/USB-4065 fails after calibration, try
 the following:

1. Check the connections and run the Calibration Executive procedure again.
2. If the calibration still fails after trying step 1, try verifying the failed
 Test Points using the DMM Soft Front Panel (SFP), which is located at Start»All Programs»National Instruments»NI-DMM»NI-DMM Soft Front Panel . For example, if the analog input (AI) failed at 10.00 VDC, input
 that value from the calibrator.
  1. Launch the DMM SFP.
  2. Using the controls on the DMM SFP, select the appropriate mode and range
 as follows:
    - mode : V DC
    - range : 10.00 V
3. Check the reading on the display.

If the reading falls outside the range of limits shown in the calibration report
 after you complete steps 1 and 2, contact NI for repair or replacement. If the
 reading falls within the limits, you can change the calibration report to indicate
 the passing value.

Note

Program Files\National
 Instruments\CalibrationExecutive\Databases\Calibration
 Reports.mdb

#### Test Limit
 Equations

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| DC Voltage | TestLimits = TestValue ± (TestValue * ppm of reading + DMM Range * ppm of range) |
| DC Current | TestLimits = TestValue ± (TestValue * ppm of reading + DMM Range * ppm of range) |
| AC Voltage | TestLimits = TestValue ± (TestValue * % of reading + DMM Range * % of range) |
| AC Current | TestLimits = TestValue ± (TestValue * % of reading + DMM Range * % of range) |
| Resistance (4-Wire and 2-Wire) | TestLimits = TestValue ± (TestValue * ppm of reading + DMM Range * ppm of range) |

Parent topic:

Calibrating Digital Multimeters (DMMs) Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure
