- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-5698 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Warmup Characterization Verify Only 30 minutes 22 minutes (+ warmup) 10 minutes (+ warmup + characterization) Test Equipment The

### Calibrating PXIe-5698 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-5698 using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure
 Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Warmup | Characterization | Verify Only |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 minutes | 22 minutes (+ warmup) | 10 minutes (+ warmup + characterization) |

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists the test equipment required for
 calibrating PXIe-5698 modules.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Power Meter | Anritsu ML2438A | All | Display resolution: ≤0.01 dB Settle per reading: 0.10% Instrumentation accuracy: <± 0.5% Reference power uncertainty: 1.2%/year Reference output VSWR: <1.04 : 1 |
| Power Sensors (x2) | Anritsu SC7413 | All | Power range: -55 dBm to -20 dBm Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Input VSWR: <150 MHz: <1.17 : 1 150 MHz to <2 GHz: <1.08 : 1 2 GHz to <12.4 GHz: <1.16 : 1 12.4 GHz to <18 GHz: <1.21 : 1 18 GHz to <26.5 GHz: <1.29 : 1 Linearity: <18 GHz: <1.8% 18 GHz to <26.5 GHz: <2.5% |
| RF source | Anritsu MG3694C Options 2B, 4, and 28B | All | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Level power: -15 dBm Harmonics: 10 MHz to ≤100 MHz:<-40 dBc >100 MHz to ≤2.2 GHz: <-50 dBc >2.2 GHz to 26.5 GHz: <-30 dBc Nonharmonic spurious: 10 MHz to ≤2.2 GHz: <-60 dBc >2.2 GHz to ≤26.5 GHz: ≤-60 dBc |
| PXI Express Chassis | PXIe-1075 or PXIe-1085 | All | — |
| PXI Express Controller | PXIe-8133 or PXIe-8135 | All | — |
| Spectrum analyzer | PXIe-5668, 26.5 GHz variant with the 765 MHz instantaneous bandwidth | Gain | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Reference level: -50 dBm to 0 dBm Amplitude accuracy: ±1.5 dB |
| 5698 to 5668R semi-rigid cable, AMP OUT to RF IN | NI PN: 159289A-01 | Verification | — |
| 2.92 mm (m)-to-2.92 mm (m) cables (24 in.) (x3) | — | — | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Insertion loss: ≤5 dB at 26.5 GHz Impedance: 50 Ω |
| 2.92 mm (m)-to-2.92 mm (m) adaptor | Anritsu K220B |  | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Impedance: 50 Ω VSWR: 10 MHz to ≤26.5 GHz:: <1.12 : 1 |
| Attenuator | Anritsu 41KC-20 | — | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.5 GHz Attenuation: 20 dB (nominal) Power rating: 2 W average Impedance: 50 Ω VSWR: 10 MHz to 12 GHz: ≤1.10 : 1 12 GHz to 18 GHz: ≤1.15 : 1 18 GHz to 26.5 GHz: ≤1.18 : 1 |
| Power splitter (two-resistor type) | Weinschel 1534 |  | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 26.9 GHz Insertion loss: ≤10.5 dB (6 dB, nominal) Power rating: 1 W Output VSWR: ≤1.35 :1 Note Ensure that the equivalent splitter output SWR meets the above requirement between 10 MHz and 26.5 GHz. |
| Torque wrench | NI 781615-01 | — | 1 N · m (9 lb · in) |

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are
 required to ensure the PXIe-5698 meets published
 specifications.

- Keep cabling as short as possible. Long cables
 and wires act as antennas, picking up extra noise
 that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections, including front
 panel connections and screws, are secure.
- Use an appropriate torque wrench or torque
 screwdriver and SMA driver bit to tighten all
 module RF connectors (SMA or 2.92 mm). NI
 recommends use of 1 N · m (9 lb. · in.).
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ±5
 °C.
- Keep the relative humidity between 10% and
 90%, noncondensing.
- Ensure that the PXI Express chassis fan speed is set to HIGH, that the fan filters, if present,
 are clean, and that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more information
 about maintaining adequate air circulation in your PXI/PXI Express chassis,
 refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document available at
 ni.com/docs .
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes
 after the chassis is powered on and NI-RFSA is
 loaded and recognizes the PXIe-5698. The warm-up
 time ensures that the PXIe-5698 and test
 instrumentation are at a stable operating
 temperature.
- In each verification procedure, insert a delay
 between configuring all devices and acquiring the
 measurement. Adjust this delay depending on the
 instruments used but make sure it is at least
 1,000 ms for the first iteration and 100 ms for
 each other iteration.
- Lock all test equipment to the REF OUT signal
 on the back of the PXI Express chassis.

#### Device Setup

1. Install the PXIe-5698 in the PXI chassis according to the instructions in the PXIe-5698
 Getting Started documentation. Note The PXIe-5698 must be installed
 in the chassis slot adjacent to and on the left side of the
 PXIe-5668R.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement &
 Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive
 procedure. Calibration Executive guides you
 through the required connections.

Parent topic:

Calibrating RF Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

## TOPIC 00190: Calibrating PXIe-5840 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-5840-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-5840-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-5840 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Self-Calibration Verify Only 25 minutes 30 minutes Test EquipmentThe following table lists the test equipment required to calibr

### Calibrating PXIe-5840 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-5840 using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Self-Calibration | Verify Only |
| --- | --- |
| 25 minutes | 30 minutes |

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists
 the test equipment required to calibrate PXIe-5840.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Minimum Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Frequency reference | Symmetricom 8040 Rubidium Frequency Standard | Verifications: Internal frequency reference Spectral purity | Frequency: 10 MHzFrequency accuracy: ≤±1E-9Output mode: sinusoid |
| Power sensor | Rohde & Schwarz NRP-Z91 with NRP-Z4 USB adapter | Test system characterization Verifications: Absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | Range: -60 dBm to +20 dBmFrequency range: 10 MHz to 6 GHz Absolute uncertainty: 0.15 dB Power linearity: <0.17 dB VSWR: <1.22:1 up to 6 GHz |
| Spectrum analyzer or vector signal analyzer | PXIe-5665 | Test system characterization Verifications: Spectral purity Output power level accuracy Output frequency response | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 12 GHz Instantaneous bandwidth: 50 MHz Phase noise at 20 kHz offset: <-125 dBm/Hz |
| Power splitter (x2) | Aeroflex/Weinschel 1593 | Test system characterization Verifications: Frequency response Absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | VSWR: ≤1.25:1 up to 18 GHz Amplitude tracking: <0.25 dB |
| 6 dB attenuator (x4) | Anritsu 41KB-6 or Mini-Circuits | Test system characterization Verifications: Frequency response Absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz VSWR: ≤1.1:1 |
| 50 Ω SMA terminator | — | Test system characterization Average noise density verification | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz VSWR: ≤1.1:1 |
| SMA (m)-to-SMA (m) cable (x3) | — | All procedures | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz Impedance: 50 Ω |
| SMA (m)-to-MMPX (m) cable (x2) | NI P/N 786000-12 | All procedures | — |
| SMA (m)-to-N (f) adapter (x3) | 33_SMA-N-50-1/1-_UE | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥23 dB |
| SMA (f)-to-N (f) adapter | Huber+Suhner 31_N-SMA-50-1/1-_UE | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥23 dB |
| 3.5 mm (m)-to-3.5 mm (m) adapter (x2) | Huber+Suhner 32_PC35-50-0-2/199_NE | Test system characterization Verifications: Frequency response Absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥30 dB |
| 3.5 mm (f)-to-3.5 mm (f) adapter | Huber+Suhner 31_PC35-50-0-1/199_UE | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥30 dB |

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and
 environmental conditions are required to ensure the PXIe-5840 meets published
 specifications.

- Ensure that the PXI chassis fan speed is set to HIGH, that the fan filters are clean, and
 that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more information about cooling,
 refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document available at
 ni.com/docs .
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes after the chassis is powered on and
 NI-RFSA/G Instrument Design Libraries are loaded and recognize the PXIe-5840.
 The warm-up time ensures that the PXIe-5840 and test instrumentation are at a
 stable operating temperature.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ±5 °C.
- Keep relative humidity between 10% and 90%, noncondensing.
- Keep cabling as short as possible. Long cables act as antennas, picking up extra
 noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the PXIe-5840, including front panel connections
 and screws, are secure.

#### Device Setup

1. Install the PXIe-5840 in the PXI chassis according to the instructions in the PXIe-5840
 Getting Started Guide, which is available at available at
 ni.com/docs .
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Perform a self-calibration before running the Verification Only procedure.
4. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

#### Self-Calibration

You can skip the
 self-calibration at the beginning of the procedure if both the PXIe-5840 and the
 external transceiver meet the following conditions:

- The device temperature is within ±5 °C of the last self-calibration
 temperature.
- The device has not been power-cycled since the last self-calibration.

Otherwise, Calibration Executive automatically performs self-calibration for
 both the PXIe-5840 and the external transceiver simultaneously. If either
 self-calibration is unsuccessful, Calibration Executive retires each
 self-calibration sequentially to avoid signal interactions between the devices. This
 can increase the test time for the automated calibration procedure.

Note

If the self-calibration of the PXIe-5840
 fails, verify that the device is meeting the test conditions, including ambient
 temperature stability and chassis fan speed, and restart the procedure. If the
 self-calibration failure cannot be resolved, the device may need to be
 repaired.

#### Fixture Characterization
 Considerations

Characterize each fixture at the beginning of the
 procedure. Calibration Executive does not support saving characterization data
 between separate procedure runs. For a characterization to maintain its validity
 during calibration, ensure that no portion of the fixture is disconnected or
 modified during the procedure run. Avoid re-torquing any part of the
 fixture.

The following figures indicate the portion of the fixtures that must
 remain intact during use.

Figure 27.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-EDEDBBF2-BEC9-4F29-98E7-73A06F945F08-a5.gif']

Figure 28.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-FF202C0E-4A80-40B3-9A1D-924FDFD75241-a5.gif']

#### RF Signal Generator

The PXIe-5840
 calibration procedure is designed to use the RF Out of the DUT to perform the
 splitter characterization and the input accuracy tests. If RF Out is not functional,
 you can use a separate PXIe-5840 module instead to complete the verification of
 these tests. Prior to starting the procedure, the operator can select a separate
 PXIe-5840 module in the setup wizard, on the DUT Selection screen.

#### Test Limit
 Equations

| Equation Type | Equations |
| --- | --- |
| Internal Frequency Reference Accuracy | As-Found Test Limit = ±[(Initial adjustment accuracy + Temperature stability) + (Aging × 1 year)] As-Left Test Limits = ±(Initial adjustment accuracy + Temperature stability) |
| 2-Year Calibration Interval | When you run the PXIe-5840 verification procedure using a 2-year interval, the Internal Frequency Reference Accuracy limit uses two years for aging instead of one year, and 0.2 dB is added to the as-found test limits for the following tests: RF Input Absolute Amplitude Accuracy RF Input Frequency Response RF Output Power Level Accuracy RF Output Frequency Response |

Parent topic:

Calibrating RF Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=pxie-5841-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00191: Calibrating PXIe-5841 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-5841-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-5841-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-5841 using Calibration Executive. This procedure supports the PXIe-5841, the PXIe-5841 Analyzer Only, and PXIe-5841 Generator Only procedures. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Adjust Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 296 App

### Calibrating PXIe-5841 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-5841 using Calibration Executive. This procedure supports the
 PXIe-5841, the PXIe-5841 Analyzer Only, and PXIe-5841 Generator Only procedures.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate
 Test Time

| Run Options | Warm-up 30 minutes1 | Self-Calibration 25-75 minutes2 | Characterization 15 minutes | As-found Verify Steps 60 minutes | Adjust Steps 250 minutes | As-left Verify Steps 60 minutes | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | X | X | X | X | — | — | 130 to 180 minutes |
| Adjust Only | X | X3 | X | — | X | — | 320 minutes |
| Verify & Adjust | X | X | X | X | X | X | 440 to 490 minutes |

| Run Options | Warm-up 30 minutes1 | Self-Calibration 25-75 minutes2 | Characterization 15 minutes | As-found Verify Steps 45 minutes | Adjust Steps 250 minutes | As-left Verify Steps 45 minutes | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | X | X | X | X | — | — | 115 to 165 minutes |
| Adjust Only | X | X3 | X | — | X | — | 320 minutes |
| Verify & Adjust | X | X | X | X | X | X | 410 to 460 minutes |

| Run Options | Warm-up 30 minutes1 | Self-Calibration 25-75 minutes2 | Characterization 15 minutes | As-found Verify Steps 15 minutes | Adjust Steps 250 minutes | As-left Verify Steps 15 minutes | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | X | X | X | X | — | — | 85 to 135 minutes |
| Adjust Only | X | X3 | X | — | X | — | 320 minutes |
| Verify & Adjust | X | X | X | X | X | X | 350 to 400 minutes |

Note

- 1 Warm-up time is based on when the DUT was powered on. Warm-up
 can be skipped if not applicable for a specific calibration run.
- 2 Self-calibration can be skipped if the PXIe-5841 devices have
 not been power-cycled and if the internal temperature is maintained to ±5 °C
 of the last self-calibration.
- 3 Self-calibration for Adjust Only is 25 minutes because only the
 external transceiver needs to be self-calibrated.

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists
 the test equipment required to calibrate PXIe-5841.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Minimum Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Frequency reference | Symmetricom 8040 Rubidium Frequency Standard | Verifications: Internal frequency reference Spectral purity | Frequency: 10 MHzFrequency accuracy: ≤±1E-9Output mode: sinusoid |
| Power sensor | Rohde & Schwarz NRP-18A | Test system characterization Verifications: Input absolute amplitude accuracy Frequency response Output power level accuracy | Range: -60 dBm to +20 dBm Frequency range: 10 MHz to 6.5 GHz Absolute uncertainty: <0.097 dBPower linearity: <0.097 dBVSWR: <1.20:1 up to 6.5 GHz |
| Vector signal analyzer | PXIe-5668R | Test system characterization RF output IMD3 functional performance test Adjustments: RF internal frequency reference LO gain | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 6.5 GHz Instantaneous bandwidth: 50 MHzPhase noise at 20 kHz offset: <-125 dBc/Hz |
| RF Signal Generator #1 | PXIe-5654 with PXIe-5696 | RF input IMD3 functional performance test Adjustment: LO gain | Output amplitude range: +5 dBm to -55 dBm Frequency range: 70 MHz to 6.5 GHz |
| RF Signal Generator #2 | PXIe-5654 with PXIe-5696* orAnritsu MG3692C Options 2A, 4 and 28A Note Do not use Option 15. | RF input IMD3 functional performance test | Output amplitude range: +5 dBm to -55 dBm Frequency range: 70 MHz to 6.5 GHz |
| Power splitter (x3) | Aeroflex/Weinschel 1593 | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: RF input power splitter assembly (x1) RF output power splitter assembly (x1) LO splitter assembly (x1) Verifications: Frequency response Input absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy Adjustments: Input absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy LO gain | VSWR: ≤1.25:1 up to 18 GHz Amplitude tracking: <0.25 dB |
| Power Combiner | Mini Circuits ZFRSC-123-S+ | Input/Output IMD3 functional performance test | Impedance: 50 Ω Frequency: DC to 12 GHzIsolation: 19.5 dB |
| 6 dB attenuator (x3) | Anritsu 41KB-6 or Mini-Circuits BW-S6-2W263+ | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: RF input power splitter assembly (x1) LO splitter assembly (x1) Used as a part of the characterization (x1): RF output power splitter assembly LO MMPX cable loss assembly Adjustments: Input absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz VSWR: ≤1.1:1 |
| 12 dB attenuator | Mini Circuits BW-S12W2+ | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: RF output power splitter assembly Verification: Output power level accuracy Adjustment: LO gain | VSWR: ≤1.25:1 Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz |
| 20 dB attenuator | Anritsu 41KC-20 | Used in the following fixture: LO MMPX cable loss assembly Adjustment: LO gain | VSWR: ≤1.1:1Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz |
| 50 Ω SMA terminator | — | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz VSWR: ≤1.1:1 |
| SMA (m)-to-SMA (m) cable (x7) | — | All procedures | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 Ω |
| SMA (m)-to-MMPX (m) cable (x3) | NI P/N 786000-12 | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: LO MMPX cable loss assembly (x1) LO splitter assembly (x1) Used in the following steps (x1): Verifications: RF input spectral purity RF output spectral purity Adjustment: Adjusting LO gain (RF IN and RF OUT) | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 Ω |
| MMPX (m)-to-BNC (m) cable (x3) | NI P/N 763771-01 | All procedures | — |
| MMCX (f)-to-SMA (f) adapter | — | Test system characterization | — |
| SMA (f)-to-N (f) adapter | Huber+Suhner 31_N-SMA-50-1/1-_UE | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥23 dB |
| SMA (m)-to-N (f) adapter (x4) | 33_SMA-N-50-1/1-_UE | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: RF input power splitter assembly (x1) RF output power splitter assembly (x1) LO splitter assembly (x1) Used in test system characterization tests (x1) | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥23 dB |
| 3.5 mm (m)-to-3.5 mm (m) adapter (x2) | Huber+Suhner 32_PC35-50-0-2/199_NE | Used in the following fixtures for test system characterization: RF input power splitter assembly (x1) RF output power splitter assembly (x1) Verifications: Frequency response Absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy Adjustments: Input absolute amplitude accuracy Output power level accuracy | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥30 dB |
| 3.5 mm (f)-to-3.5 mm (f) adapter | Huber+Suhner 31_PC35-50-0-1/199_UE | Test system characterization | Frequency range: DC to 6.5 GHz Impedance: 50 ΩReturn loss: ≥30 dB |
| External transceiver | PXIe-5841 | Test system characterization Adjustments/Verifications: Input absolute amplitude accuracy Frequency response Power level accuracy | Frequency range: 10 MHz to 6.5 GHz RF input reference range: -40 dB to 20 dB RF output power range: -20 dBm to 4 dBm Digital gain linearity:<0.054 dB |
| Chassis | PXIe-1095 | All procedures | — |

Note

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and
 environmental conditions are required to ensure the PXIe-5841 meets published
 specifications.

- Ensure that the PXI chassis fan speed is set to HIGH, that the fan filters are
 clean, and that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more information
 about cooling, refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document
 available at ni.com/docs .
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes after the chassis is powered on and
 NI-RFSA/G Instrument Design Libraries are loaded and recognize the PXIe-5841.
 The warm-up time ensures that the PXIe-5841 and test instrumentation are at a
 stable operating temperature.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ±5 °C.
- Keep relative humidity between 10% and 90%, noncondensing.
- Keep cabling as short as possible. Long cables act as antennas, picking up extra
 noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the PXIe-5841, including front panel connections
 and screws, are secure.

#### Device Setup

1. Install the PXIe-5841 in the PXI chassis according to the instructions in the
 PXIe-5841 Getting Started Guide, which is available at available at
 ni.com/docs .
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Perform a self-calibration before running the Verification Only procedure.
4. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.
  1. On the DUT Selection page, designate the external transceiver in the
 NI 5841 External Transceiver drop-down
 list.
  2. Select the DUT checkbox for the device you want
 to calibrate and de-select the DUT checkbox for
 the device you designated as the external transceiver.
5. Connect the reference frequency standard to the DUT, standard PXIe-5841 devices,
 and other standards.

#### Self-Calibration

You can skip the
 self-calibration at the beginning of the procedure if both the PXIe-5841 and the
 external transceiver meet the following conditions:

- The device temperature is within ±5 °C of the last self-calibration
 temperature.
- The device has not been power cycled since the last self-calibration.

Otherwise, Calibration Executive automatically performs self-calibration for
 both the PXIe-5841 and the external transceiver simultaneously. If either
 self-calibration is unsuccessful, Calibration Executive retries each
 self-calibration sequentially to avoid signal interactions between the devices. This
 can increase the test time for the automated calibration procedure.

If the
 self-calibration of the PXIe-5841 fails, verify that the device is meeting the test
 conditions, including ambient temperature stability and chassis fan speed, and
 restart the procedure. Additionally, NI recommends performing an Adjust Only run
 to resolve self-calibration errors. If the self-calibration failure cannot be
 resolved, the device may need to be repaired.

For Adjust Only run mode,
 Calibration Executive performs self-calibration of the external transceiver only at
 the beginning of the procedure.

#### External Transceiver IMD3 Failure
 Consideration

Calibration Executive executes the IMD3 characterization of
 the external transceiver at the beginning of the calibration procedure. These
 characterization results are not shown on the calibration report. The external
 transceiver must pass the IMD3 characterization before you can use it for
 calibration. If the external transceiver device fails the IMD3 characterization
 repeatedly, repair the device or replace it with another module.

#### Fixture Characterization
 Considerations

Characterize each fixture at the beginning of the
 procedure. Calibration Executive does not support saving characterization data
 between separate procedure runs. For a characterization to maintain its validity
 during calibration, ensure that no portion of the fixture is disconnected or
 modified during the procedure run. Avoid re-torquing any part of the
 fixture.

The following figures indicate the portion of the fixtures that must
 remain intact during use.

Figure 29.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-2533706D-83CE-4C12-ADBD-B71DB6DC00A6-a5.png']

Figure 30.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-4D85D294-0F51-4A81-8130-1AF06FA1FFA7-a5.png']

Figure 31.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-88679175-5FB6-45A8-B97D-2F361FF016A8-a5.png']

Figure 32.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-5E97BB20-013E-404E-8A95-4CFF8B625E48-a5.png']

#### Adjustment Considerations

Perform all
 adjustments steps, including the Adjust Self Calibrate step, without significant
 idle time between connection changes.

If you encounter an error during an
 adjustment step, re-run Adjust Only to resolve the problem. If the adjustment
 step repeatedly causes an error, repair the PXIe-5841 or replace it with another
 module.

#### Test
 Limit Equations

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| Internal Frequency Reference Accuracy | As-found Test Limit = ±[(Initial adjustment accuracy + Temperature stability) + (Aging × 1 year)] As-left Test Limits = ±(Initial adjustment accuracy + Temperature stability) |
| 2-Year Calibration Interval | When you run the PXIe-5841 verification procedure using a 2-year interval, the Internal Frequency Reference Accuracy limit uses two years for aging instead of one year, and 0.2 dB is added to the as-found test limits for the following tests: RF Input Absolute Amplitude Accuracy RF Input Frequency Response RF Output Power Level Accuracy RF Output Frequency Response |

Parent topic:

Calibrating RF Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure

## TOPIC 00192: Calibrating PXIe-5842 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-5842-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-5842-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-5842 using Calibration Executive. For general information on calibrating the PXIe-5842, see PXIe-5842 VST3 Calibration Procedure on ni.com/docs. The PXIe-5842 composite device consists of the PXIe-5655 and the PXIe-5842 modules. Therefore, the calibration of a PXIe-5842 module req

### Calibrating PXIe-5842 Using Calibration
 Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-5842 using Calibration Executive.

Note

PXIe-5842 VST3 Calibration Procedure

ni.com/docs

The PXIe-5842 composite device consists of the PXIe-5655 and the PXIe-5842 modules.
 Therefore, the calibration of a PXIe-5842 module requires an associated PXIe-5655
 module. When you calibrate the PXIe-5842, you can choose from multiple calibration
 procedures.

Note

PXIe-5842 with PXIe-5655

- The PXIe-5842 module that you want to calibrate is the only
 PXIe-5842 module that you use with the PXIe-5655 module in the
 system.
- You want to calibrate the PXIe-5842 module and the PXIe-5655 module
 together.

PXIe-5842 with PXIe-5655 (4 GHz BW)

Standard

4 GHz Bandwidth

- The PXIe-5842 module that you want to calibrate is the only
 PXIe-5842 module that you use with the PXIe-5655 module in the
 system.
- You want to calibrate the PXIe-5842 module and the PXIe-5655
 module together.

PXIe-5655

- Your system contains multiple PXIe-5842 modules that are associated
 with the PXIe-5655 module.
- You want to calibrate the PXIe-5842 modules in your system with the
 PXIe-5655 module as a standard after you run this procedure.

PXIe-5842 Module

PXIe-5655

1. Run the PXIe-5655 calibration procedure to calibrate the
 PXIe-5655 module.
2. Run the PXIe-5842 Module calibration procedure for each
 PXIe-5842 module separately. Note Ensure that you
 select the PXIe-5655 as a standard during setup.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Note

PXIe-5842 with PXIe-5655 (4 GHz BW)

#### Approximate Test Times

Note

The test times include the calibration of both the PXIe-5842 and the PXIe-5655.
 Test times do not include the 30 minutes of warm-up time.

PXIe-5842 Module

- Verify Only test times are five minutes shorter.
- Adjust Only test times are 85 minutes shorter.
- Verify & Adjust test times are
 95
 minutes shorter.

#### Approximate Test Times of the PXIe-5842 and PXIe-5655 at 26.5 GHz Frequency
 Range

| 2 GHz | 3 hours 5 minutes | 3 hours 40 minutes | 8 hours 35 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 2 hours 55 minutes | 3 hours 35 minutes | 8 hours 10 minutes |
| 500 MHz | 2 hours 45 minutes | 3 hours 30 minutes | 7 hours 45 minutes |

| 2 GHz | 2 hours 20 minutes | 3 hours 25 minutes | 6 hours 50 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 2 hours 10 minutes | 3 hours 20 minutes | 6 hours 25 minutes |

| Bandwidth | Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust |

| 2 GHz | 2 hours 20 minutes | 3 hours 20 minutes | 6 hours 45 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 2 hours 10 minutes | 3 hours 15 minutes | 6 hours 20 minutes |
| 500 MHz | 2 hours 5 minutes | 3 hours 10 minutes | 6 hours 5 minutes |

 Range

| 1 GHz | 2 hours 35 minutes | 3 hours 10 minutes | 7 hours 5 minutes |

#### Approximate Test Times of the PXIe-5842 and PXIe-5655 at 12 GHz Frequency
 Range

| 1 GHz | 2 hours 15 minutes | 2 hours 55 minutes | 6 hours 10 minutes |

| 500 MHz | 1 hour 40 minutes | 2 hours 40 minutes | 4 hours 45 minutes |

#### Approximate Test Times of the PXIe-5842 and PXIe-5655 at 8 GHz Frequency
 Range

| 2 GHz | 2 hours 0 minutes | 2 hours 45 minutes | 5 hours 30 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 2 hours 0 minutes | 2 hours 45 minutes | 5 hours 30 minutes |
| 500 MHz | 1 hour 55 minutes | 2 hours 45 minutes | 5 hours 20 minutes |

| Bandwidth | Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust |

| 2 GHz | 1 hour 40 minutes | 2 hours 35 minutes | 4 hours 40 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 1 hour 40 minutes | 2 hours 35 minutes | 4 hours 40 minutes |
| 500 MHz | 1 hour 35 minutes | 2 hours 35 minutes | 4 hours 30 minutes |

| Bandwidth | Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 GHz | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours 40 minutes | 4 hours 55 minutes |
| 1 GHz | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours 40 minutes | 4 hours 55 minutes |
| 500 MHz | 1 hour 40 minutes | 2 hours 40 minutes | 4 hours 45 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Minimum Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Chassis | PXIe-1095, NI part number 785971-01 | All | Timing sync option |
| Controller | PXIe-8881, NI part number 786636-01 | All | 16 GB RAM and 500 GB SSD |
| Spectrum analyzer | Rohde & Schwarz FSWP26 | RF input third-order intermodulation verification RF output average noise density verification RF output harmonic spurs verification RF output third-order intermodulation verification | SeeTable 314. Options for the Rohde & Schwarz FSWP26 for options needed. |
| Power sensor #1 | Rohde & Schwarz NRP33S | Receiver fixture characterization Cable loss characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input power linearity accuracy verification Input frequency response verification | — |
| Power sensor #2 | Rohde & Schwarz NRP33S | Receiver fixture characterization Cable loss characterization RF output absolute amplitude verification RF output frequency response verification Reference clock gain adjustment RF transmission LO output adjustment RF receiver LO output adjustment | — |
| Signal generator (2x) | Rohde & Schwarz SMA100B 31.8 GHzSee Table 315. Options for the SMA-100B for options needed. | Receiver fixture characterization Cable loss characterization RF input third-order intermodulation verification RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input power linearity accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification | — |
| 10 MHz Rb Reference standard | Microchip 8040 | All | Reference clock routing |
| GPIB to USB Adapter (x3) | NI part number 783368-01 | All | — |
| Local oscillatorNote The local oscillator is part of the DUT only for the PXIe-5842 with PXIe-5655 calibration procedure. | PXIe-5655, NI part number 786586-02 | All | — |
| Semi-rigid cable, PXIe-5842 REF: IN to PXIe-5655 REF OUT, 2.92 mm to 2.92 mm | NI part number 136103A-01 | All | — |
| Semi-rigid cable, PXIe-5842 RF OUT: LO IN to PXIe-5655 LO 0 OUT, 2.92 mm to 2.92 mm | NI part number 136104A-01 | All | — |
| Semi-rigid cable, PXIe-5842 RF IN: LO IN to PXIe-5655 LO 1 OUT, 2.92 mm to 2.92 mm | NI part number 136106A-01 | All | — |
| SMA (m) to BNC (m) cable | Fairview Microwave FMC0208315-36 | All | Length: ≤1 m or 36 in. Reference clock routing |
| BNC (m) to BNC (m) cable (3x) | Fairview Microwave FMC0808058-36 | All | Length: ≤1 m or 36 in. Reference clock routing |
| 3.5 mm (m) to (m) cable (5x) | Maury Microwave SP-35MM-36-LP | Receiver fixture characterization Cable loss characterization RF input third-order intermodulation verification RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input power linearity accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification RF output average noise density verification RF output harmonic spurs verification RF output third-order intermodulation verification | Length: ≤1 m or 36 in. |
| 3.5 mm power splitter (two-resistor type) | Keysight 11667B | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input power linearity accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification | VSWR: ≤1.22 |
| LF Power combiner | Mini Circuits ZFRSC-123-S+ | RF input third-order intermodulation verification | Frequency: DC to 12 GHz |
| HF 2.92 mm Power combiner | Mini Circuits ZC2PD-K0144+ | RF input third-order intermodulation verification | Frequency: 1 GHz to 26 GHz |
| 45-degree SMA (m) to SMA (f) adapter | CentricRF C3243 | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input power linearity accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification | VSWR: ≤1.15 |
| 3.5 mm (f) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter (2x) | Maury Microwave CC-A-35-FF | Receiver fixture characterization Cable loss characterization RF output average noise density verification RF output harmonic spurs verification RF output third-order intermodulation verification | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (m) adapter | Maury Microwave CC-A-35-MM | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter | Maury Microwave CC-A-35-MF | RF transmission LO output adjustment RF receiver LO output adjustment | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| 3.5 mm (m) 50 Ω terminator | Pasternack PE6TR1109 | RF input average noise density verification | VSWR: ≤1.15 |
| Laboratory Mini Scissor Lift Jack | — | Support power sensors | — |

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| FSWP26 | Phase noise analyzer and VCO tester 1 MHz to 26.5 GHz optionally with cross correlation and spectrum analyzer. |
| FSWP-B1 | Signal and spectrum analyzer 10 Hz to 26 GHz for Rohde & Schwarz FSWP26 (hardware option, retrofittable in factory). |
| FSWP-B4 | High stability OCXO with low phase noise (hardware option). |
| FSWP-B24 | RF preamplifier, 100 kHz to 26 GHz for Rohde & Schwarz FSWP26 option. Rohde & Schwarz FSWP-B1 required (hardware option). |
| FSWP-B61 | Cross correlation for phase noise analyzer, 1 MHz to 26 GHz for Rohde & Schwarz FSWP26 option (hardware option). |

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SMAB-B131 | Frequency range: 8 kHz to 31.8 GHz, not installable post factory (hardware option). |
| SMAB-B35 | High output power 31.8/40 GHz, frequency option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B131/B140/ B140N) and 3HU option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B93) required, not installable post factory (hardware option). |
| SMAB-K36 | Ultra high output power 31.8/40 GHz, frequency option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B131/B140(N)) and high-power option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B35) required (software license). |
| SMAB-B86 | Remote control GPIB/USB, retrofittable in Rohde & Schwarz service w/ UCS (hardware Option). |
| SMA100B | Signal Generator Base Unit, frequency option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B103/B106/B112/B120/B131/B140/B140N/B150/B150N/B167/B167N) and height unit option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B92/B93) required. |

#### Test
 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required
 to ensure that the PXIe-5842 meets published specifications:

- Maintain an ambient temperature of 22.5 °C ± 2.5 °C .
- During adjustment, the internal device temperature must maintain ±2 °C stability for the duration of the
 adjustment.
- Keep relative humidity between 10% and 90%, noncondensing.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes after powering on the chassis and
 NI-RFSA Instrument Design Libraries are loaded and recognize
 the PXIe-5842. The warm-up time ensures that the PXIe-5842 and test
 instrumentation are at a stable operating temperature.
- Allow proper warm up time for all instrumentation that you use for verification
 steps and adjustment steps. See the respective specifications documents for the
 instrumentation for more details.
- Use a torque wrench appropriate for the type of RF connector that you are using.
 NI recommends a 0.565 N · m ( 5 lb · in. ) wrench for SMA connectors. NI
 recommends a 0.90 N · m ( 8 lb · in. ) wrench for 2.92 mm connectors.
- Set the fan speed of the PXI/PXI Express chassis to AUTO .
 Ensure that the fan filters are clean and that the empty slots contain filler
 panels. For more information about cooling, refer to the Maintain
 Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document at
 ni.com/docs .

#### Device Setup

1. Install the PXIe-5842 along with the PXIe-5655 LO in the PXI Express
 chassis.
2. Verify that all connections, including front panel connections and screws, are
 secure.
3. Configure the PXIe-5655 as the Associated LO for the PXIe-5842
 in Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
4. Connect the frequency reference source to the REF IN 
 connector on the PXI Express chassis with a standard SMA (m) to BNC (m) cable.
 Connect the REF IN connections of all instruments to the
 same reference source.
5. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure. Calibration Executive guides you
 through the setup of the required connections.

#### Hardware Licensing Considerations

Calibration Executive automatically
 detects the licensed functionality that is associated with the PXIe-5842 under test.
 The verification test points are limited to functionality that the license of the
 device includes.

#### External SMA-100B IMD3 Failure Considerations

At the beginning of the
 procedure, Calibration Executive characterizes the IMD3 performance of the two
 SMA-100B signal generators and associated fixtures that you use for the PXIe-5842 RF
 Input IIP3 verification. Calibration Executive does not include these
 characterization results in the calibration report. The IMD3 characterization must
 pass to proceed with the PXIe-5842 verification. If the IMD3 characterization fails
 repeatedly, check the connections and evaluate if the devices or fixtures require
 repair or replacement.

#### Fixture Characterization Considerations

The RF input and output tests
 require fixtures that are characterized at the beginning of the procedure.
 Calibration Executive does not support saving characterization data between separate
 procedure runs. For a characterization to maintain its validity during calibration,
 ensure that no portion of the fixture is disconnected or modified during the
 procedure run. Avoid re-torquing any part of the fixture.

The following
 figures indicate the portion of the fixtures that must remain intact during
 use:

Figure 33.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-931C39BF-4641-4B6A-BD47-E262CF3C18C1-a5.png']

1. 45-degree SMA (m) to SMA (f) adapter
2. 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (m) adapter
3. 3.5 mm power splitter (two-resistor type)
4. Power Sensor #2
5. 3.5 mm (m) to (m) cable
6. Signal Generator #1

Figure 34.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-AF94D357-DB48-4288-B926-1FB99DF04049-a5.png']

1. Power Sensor #1
2. 3.5 mm (f) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter
3. Receiver Fixture from Figure 33.Initial Connection for
 Receiver Fixture Characterization
4. Signal Generator #1

Figure 35.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-62FEE36A-C067-4F10-9E05-5A9FFCBDCCA8-a5.png']

1. Power sensor #1
2. 3.5 mm (f) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter (2x)
3. 36" 3.5 mm (m) to (m) cable (2x)
4. 45-degree SMA (m) to (f) adapter
5. 3.5 mm (m) to (m) adapter
6. 3.5 mm power splitter (2 resistor)
7. Power sensor #2
8. Signal generator #1

1. Assemble the fixture according to the connection image that Calibration
 Executive displays in the dialog box.
2. Calibration Executive performs the first characterization run.
3. Disassemble the fixture when prompted by the dialog box.
4. Reassemble the fixture according to the connection image that Calibration
 Executive displays in the dialog box.
5. Calibration Executive performs the second characterization run. Note A dialog box shows the results of the two runs on
 a graph.
6. Click Continue on the dialog box.
7. Calibration Executive compares the results of the two characterization
 runs.
8. If the comparison between the measurements is within test limits,
 Calibration Executive proceeds to the next step automatically.
9. If the comparison between the measurements is outside of test limits, a
 dialog box appears.
10. Check the dialog box to see which characterization point causes the
 deviation.
11. Click Retry on the dialog box to re-run the
 characterization procedure.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-622AAD8E-8608-463B-887B-5BF3F9B2FCD7-a5.png']

#### Adjustment Considerations

Adjustment Only

- The device does not maintain stability.
- You encounter an error during an adjustment step.

Parent topic:

Calibrating RF Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related information:

- PXIe-5842 VST3 Calibration Procedure

Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Adjust Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 316 Approximate Test Time for the PXIe-5860 Run Options Warm-Up Self-Calibration Characterization As-Found Verify St

### Calibrating PXIe-5860 Using Calibration
 Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-5860 using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Adjust Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Run Options | Warm-Up | Self-Calibration | Characterization | As-Found Verify Steps | Adjust Steps | As-Left Verify Steps | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | 30 minutes | 7 minutes | 20 minutes | 70 minutes | — | — | 127 minutes |
| Adjust Only | 30 minutes | — | 20 minutes | — | 50 minutes | — | 100 minutes |
| Verify & Adjust | 30 minutes | 7 minutes | 20 minutes | 70 minutes | 50 minutes | 70 minutes | 247 minutes |

#### Test
 Equipment

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Where Used | Minimum Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Chassis | PXIe-1095, NI part number 785971-01 | All | Timing sync option |
| Power sensor #1 | Rohde & Schwarz NRP33S | Receiver fixture characterization Generator adapter characterization RF output absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF output frequency response verification RF output gain adjustment | — |
| Power sensor #2 | Rohde & Schwarz NRP33S | Receiver fixture characterization RF output absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF output frequency response verification RF output gain adjustment | — |
| Signal generator | Rohde & Schwarz SMA100B 31.8 GHzSee Table 318. Options for the SMA-100B for options needed. | Receiver fixture characterization Generator adapter characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification RF input gain adjustment | — |
| 10 MHz Rb reference standard | Microchip 8040 | All | Reference clock routing |
| GPIB to USB adapter | NI part number 783368-01 | All | — |
| SMA (m) to BNC (m) cable | Fairview Microwave FMC0208315-36 | All | Length: ≤1 m (36 in.) Reference clock routing |
| BNC (m) to BNC (m) cable | Fairview Microwave FMC0808058-36 | All | Length: ≤1 m (36 in.) Reference clock routing |
| 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (m) cable | Maury Microwave SP-35MM-36-LP | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification RF input gain adjustment | Length: ≤1 m (36 in.) Frequency: ≥26.5 GHz VSWR: ≤1.25 |
| 3.5 mm power splitter (two-resistor type) | Keysight 11667B | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification RF input gain adjustment | VSWR: ≤1.22 |
| 3.5 mm (f) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter | Maury Microwave CC-A-35-FF | Receiver fixture characterization | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (m) adapter | Maury Microwave CC-A-35-MM | Receiver fixture characterization RF input absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF input frequency response verification RF input gain adjustment | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| 3.5 mm (m) to 3.5 mm (f) adapter | Maury Microwave CC-A-53-MF | Generator adapter characterization RF output absolute amplitude accuracy verification RF output frequency response verification RF output gain adjustment | VSWR: ≤1.12 |
| Laboratory Mini Scissor Lift Jack | — | Supports power sensors | — |
| 10 dB attenuator | ANRITSU 41KB-10, 10 dB Precision Attenuator | Generator adapter characterization | — |

| Option | Description | Part Number |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SMAB-B112 or compatible option that supports 50 MHz to 9 GHz | Frequency range: 8 kHz to 31.8 GHz, not installable post factory (hardware option) | 1420.8888.02 |
| SMAB-B86 | Remote control GPIB/USB, retrofittable in Rohde & Schwarz service w/ UCS (hardware Option) | 1420.6562.02 |
| SMA100B | Signal Generator Base Unit, frequency option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B103/B106/B112/B120/B131/B140/B140N/B150/B150N/B167/B167N) and height unit option (Rohde & Schwarz SMAB-B92/B93) required | 1419.8888.02 |

#### Test
 Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required
 to ensure the PXIe-5860 meets published specifications.

- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ± 5 °C .
- During adjustment, the internal device temperature must maintain ±2 °C stability for the duration of the
 adjustment.
- Keep relative humidity between 10% and 90%, noncondensing.
- Allow a warm-up time of at least 30 minutes after powering on the chassis
 and
 the NI-RFSA Instrument Design Libraries are loaded and
 recognize the PXIe-5860. The warm-up time ensures that the PXIe-5860 and test
 instrumentation are at a stable operating temperature.
- Allow proper warm up time for all instrumentation that you use for verification
 steps and adjustment steps. See the respective specifications documents for the
 instrumentation for more details.
- Use a torque wrench appropriate for the type of RF connector that you are using.
 NI recommends a 0.565 N · m ( 5 lb · in. ) wrench for SMA
 connectors.
 NI recommends a 0.90 N · m ( 8 lb · in. ) wrench for 2.92 mm connectors.
- Ensure that the PXI/PXI Express chassis fan speed is set to AUTO, that the fan
 filters are clean, and that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more
 information about cooling, refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note
 to Users document available at
 ni.com/docs .

#### Device
 Setup

1. Install the PXIe-5860 in the PXI Express chassis.
2. Verify that all connections, including front panel connections and screws, are
 secure.
3. Connect the frequency reference source to the REF IN connector on the PXI
 Express chassis with a standard SMA (m) to BNC (m) cable. Connect all
 instruments REF IN connections to the same reference source.
4. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure. Calibration Executive guides you
 through the required connections.

#### Fixture
 Characterization Considerations

The RF input and output tests require
 fixtures that are characterized at the beginning of the procedure. Calibration
 Executive does not support saving characterization data between separate procedure
 runs. For a characterization to maintain its validity during calibration, ensure
 that no portion of the fixture is disconnected or modified during the procedure run.
 Avoid re-torquing any part of the fixture.

The following figures indicate the
 portion of the fixtures that must remain intact during use.

Figure 36.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-E5D61772-CA57-413B-BA12-44EA65A5DDA2-a5.png']

 mm (f) adapter

 mm (m) adapter

 sensor
 #1

 Calibration Executive
 displays
 in the dialog box.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=pxie-6570-6571-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00194: Calibrating PXIe-6570/6571 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-6570-6571-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-6570-6571-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-6570, PXIe-6571 and PXIe-6571 (8CH) using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 173 PXIe-6570/6571 Test times Warmup Verify Only Verify & Adjust 30 minutes 40 minutes 115 minute

### Calibrating PXIe-6570/6571 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-6570,
 PXIe-6571
 and PXIe-6571 (8CH) using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate
 Test Time

| Warmup | Verify Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 minutes | 40 minutes | 115 minutes |

| Warmup | Verify Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 minutes | 6 minutes | 18 minutes |

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists
 the test equipment required for calibrating the PXIe-6570/6571.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Specifications | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Chassis* | PXIe-1095 | All | 82 W of power, cooling |
| SMU (2 channels) | PXIe-4143 | DC Accuracy | — |
| Power supply | PXI-4110 | Timing Accuracy◊ | Programmable source, 3.3 V, 60 mA |
| DMM (x2) | PXI-4071 | DC Accuracy | 7.5 digits voltage |
| NI 657x DC calibration fixture | NI P/N 790411-01This calibration fixture has limited support and availability. | DC Accuracy | — |
| NI 657x Timing calibration fixture | NI P/N 540163A-026This calibration fixture has limited support and availability. | Timing accuracy◊ | — |
| Switch | PXIe-2529 | DC Accuracy | — |
| Oscilloscope** | PXIe-5162 (4 CH) | Timing Accuracy◊ | — |
| Reference clock | PXIe-6674T | Timing Accuracy◊ | 10 MHz reference clock Accuracy: ±25 ppm |
| Timing calibration fixture cabling | 12-inch SMA Cables (x2):NI P/N 763777-01 SMA(f)-to-BNC(m) Adapters (x2)Cable FerriteSMA-to-SMB Cable | Timing Accuracy◊ | See instructions below for connection details |
| Timing Calibration Fixture Adapter‡ | NI P/N 171043A-01This calibration fixture adapter has limited support and availability. | Timing Accuracy◊ | — |
| I2C controller | USB-8452USB cable with ferrites | Timing Accuracy◊ | — |
| VHDCI-to-VHDCI 68-pin cable | SHC68-C68-D4, NI P/N 152870-01 | DC Accuracy | 3 GHz |

Note

- *The PXIe-6571 requires a chassis that can provide 82 W of power and slot
 cooling.
- **The Oscilloscope standard cannot be used in manual mode for the
 PXIe-6570/6571 procedure.
- † The timing calibration fixture is sensitive to electrostatic
 discharge (ESD).
- ‡The
 timing calibration fixture adapter is required for the PXIe-6571 to perform
 the timing calibration procedure.
- ◊Timing accuracy equipment is not required for PXIe-6571 (8CH).

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and
 environmental conditions are required to ensure the PXIe-6570/6571 meets published
 specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act
 as antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections, including front panel connections, are secure.
- Use shielded VHDCI cables when connecting to the PXIe-6570/6571 front
 panel.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 ±5 °C. The device temperature will be
 greater than the ambient temperature.
- The ambient temperature variation during adjustment and final verification must
 be within ±1 °C.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.
- Allow recommended warm-up time for all of the instruments and equipment
 according to the manufacturer instructions.
- Use a torque wrench appropriate for the type of RF connector that you are using.
 NI recommends a 0.565 N · m (5 lb · in.) wrench for SMA connectors.
- If using a PXIe-1085 chassis to calibrate the PXIe-6570, ensure that the PXIe-1085 chassis
 fan speed is set to HIGH, the fan filters are removed, and the empty slots
 contain PXI chassis slot blockers and filler panels. For more information, refer
 to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document available at
 ni.com/docs .
- If using a PXIe-1095 chassis to calibrate the PXie-6570 or PXIe-6571, ensure the PXIe-1095
 cooling profile is set to 58W/82W and the fan mode is set to Auto. Ensure the
 empty slots contain PXI chassis slot blockers and filler panels. For more
 information, refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document
 available at ni.com/docs .

#### Device Setup

1. For PXIe-6571 modules only, verify the slot to the right of the PXIe-6571 to be
 tested is populated with the Timing Calibration Fixture Adapter.
2. Install the PXIe-6570/6571 in the chassis according to the instructions in the PXIe-6570
 Getting Started Guide or the PXIe-6571 Getting Started
 Guide .
3. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
4. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.

#### Timing
 Calibration
 Setup

Note

1. Connect the USB-8452 I2C controller and PXI-4110 DC power supply to the timing calibration
 fixture as shown in the diagrams. The cabling to connect the components is
 described in Test Equipment .
2. Remove screws as indicated for the module being tested: Note Save the screws and put them
 back into the PXIe-6570/6571 front panel when you are finished with timing
 calibration. 
 PXIe-6570: Remove three of the four screws from the
 front panel so the timing calibration fixture can be screwed onto the
 PXIe-6570 module. Leave the screw below the PXI ejector handle in place. 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-57DB1527-DD43-4C11-9DC2-E90FC8845CA0-a5.png'] 
 PXIe-6571: Remove the top screw from the front panel
 so the timing calibration fixture can be screwed onto the PXIe-6571 module.
 Leave the screw below the PXI ejector handle in place. Verify the timing
 calibration fixture adapter is populated in the slot to the right of the
 PXIe-6571 being tested. 
 [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-BA90FC45-BBE1-40F9-9E5F-964043CE9564-a5.png']
3. Attach the Timing Calibration Fixture to the front of the PXIe-6570/PXIe-6571
 device. Hand tighten the screws on the Timing Calibration Fixture.

#### DC
 Calibration Setup

The DC calibration of the PXIe-6570/6571 uses an NI 2529
 switch connected to a custom fixture that is used to properly route the signals from
 the PXIe-6570/6571 and to each of the standards used during calibration. Refer to
 *Switch Maintenance* for details on properly maintaining the NI 2529
 switch. When prompted by Calibration Executive, connect the fixture to the
 instruments and device as shown in the following diagram. Ensure that you have the
 banana cables connected to the correct DMM that you selected during setup.

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-74F06529-8BBF-472F-BDD2-C026E2B62F51-a5.png']

#### Switch
 Maintenance

To ensure proper operation of the switches used in this
 procedure, you must periodically test the performance of your switch. Refer to
 *NI 2503/2529 Switch Performance Test* for instructions on validating
 the performance of the NI 2529 used in this procedure.

#### Test Limit
 Equations

The following As Found test limits are derived from the
 published specifications. The As Left test limits are guard-banded according to
 manufacturer requirements.

Timing Accuracy Under Calibration
 Conditions

Note

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| Comparator Voltage Level Accuracy Verification Limit | Test Value ± 25 mV |
| Driver Voltage Level Accuracy Limit | Test Value ± 15 mV |
| PPMU Force Current Accuracy Limit | Test Value ± (Current Range * 1%) |
| PPMU Force Voltage Accuracy Limit | Test Value ± 15 mV |
| PPMU Measure Current (Zone 1) Accuracy Limit | 1 * (SMU Current Reading) ± (Current Range * 1%) |
| PPMU Measure Current (Zone 2) Accuracy Limit | -1 * (SMU Current Reading) ± (Current Range * Test Point Accuracy)Note Use 1% test point accuracy for the 1.25 V test points, and 1.5% for all others.Note For test limits that are set based on the SMU Current Reading or the DMM reading, the actual reading that is used to set the limits is determined by calculating the mid-point between the upper and lower limit. |
| PPMU Measure Voltage Accuracy Limit | (DMM Reading) ± 5 mV |

Parent topic:

Calibrating High-Speed Digital I/O Devices Using Calibration Executive

Related concepts:

- Launching a Calibration Procedure
- NI 2503/2529 Switch Performance Test

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=pxie-6614-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00195: Calibrating PXIe-6614 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-6614-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-6614-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-6614 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Verify Only Verify & Adjust 3 minutes 9 minutes Test EquipmentThe following table lists the test equipment required to calibrate

### Calibrating PXIe-6614 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-6614 using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- |
| 3 minutes | 9 minutes |

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists
 the test equipment required to calibrate the PXIe-6614.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 10 MHz rubidium frequency standard clock or better* | Symmetricom 8040C or Datum 8040 | ±0.75 ppb† accuracy or better |
| Shielded 68-conductor cable | Sh68-68-D1 | Terminated with 68-pin female 0.050 series D-type connector |
| I/O connector block | BNC-2121, SCB-68A, or any 68-pin breakout box | — |
| BNC male-to-BNC male cable | — | 50 Ω |
| 75 Ω BNC male to 50 Ω BNC female adapter | — | 50 to 75 Ω |

Note

- *Allow adequate time for the clock to warm up prior to calibration.
- † 1 ppb = 1/1000,000,000

#### Test Conditions

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires
 act as antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device, including front panel
 connections, are secure.
- Use shielded coaxial cables or shielded copper wire for all cable
 connections to the device. Use twisted-pair wire to eliminate noise and
 thermal offsets.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 23 °C ± 5°C.
- Keep relative humidity between 5% and 95%, non-condensing.
- Allow a warm up time of at least two hours to ensure that the measurement
 circuitry is at a stable operating temperature.

#### Device Setup

1. Power off the PXI Express chassis, and install the PXIe-6614 in an available
 slot.
2. Power on the chassis and launch MAX.
3. Configure the device identifier and select Self-Test 
 to ensure that the device is working properly.
4. Connect the PXIe-6614 to the I/O connect or block using the shielded
 68-conductor cable.
5. Connect the clock source to PFI 39 on the PXIe-6614.

#### Test
 Limit Equations

The following test limits are derived from the published
 specifications.

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| Clock Accuracy Verification and Adjustment | Frequency = 100 MHz Temperature and 1-year drift = 75 ppb Retrace error = 10 ppb Calibration measurement accuracy = 4.25 ppbAs-found Limit = Frequency * (1 ± (Temperature and 1-year drift + Retrace error)) = 100 MHz * (1 ± (75 ppb + 10 ppb)) = ± 8.5 HzAs-left Limit = Frequency * (1 ± Calibration measurement accuracy) = 100 MHz * (1 ± 4.25 ppb) = ± 0.425 Hz |

Parent topic:

Calibrating Counter/Timer Devices Using Calibration Executive

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=pxie-6674t-calibration.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00196: Calibrating PXIe-6674T Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `pxie-6674t-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/pxie-6674t-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the PXIe-6674T using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time Warmup Verify Only Verify & Adjust 24 hours 1.5 minutes 7 minutes Test EquipmentThe following table lists the test equipment re

### Calibrating PXIe-6674T Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the PXIe-6674T using Calibration Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Warmup | Verify Only | Verify & Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 24 hours | 1.5 minutes | 7 minutes |

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists
 the test equipment required for calibrating the PXIe-6674T.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 10 MHz clock source | Symmetricom/Datum 8040 | Accurate to within 0.75 parts-per-billion (ppb) |
| BNC-SMA cable | — | — |
| PXI Express chassis | PXIe-1062Q, PXIe-1075 | Any PXI Express chassis meets requirements. |

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and
 environmental conditions are required to ensure the PXIe-6674T meets published
 specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act
 as antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the device, including front panel connections,
 are secure.
- Maintain an ambient temperature of 25 °C ±5 °C.
- Keep relative humidity below 80%.
- Ensure the PXIe-6674T has been continuously powered on for at least
 24 hours.
- Use shielded copper wire for all cable connections to the device. Use
 twisted-pairs wire to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- Ensure that the PXI chassis fan speed is set to HIGH, that the fan filters are
 clean, and that the empty slots contain filler panels. For more information,
 refer to the Maintain Forced-Air Cooling Note to Users document
 available at ni.com/docs .

#### Device Setup

1. Install the PXIe-6674T board into any PXI Express slot of your PXI
 Express chassis . Refer to the NI PXIe-6674T Installation
 Guide for more details.
2. Power on the PXI Express chassis.
3. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
4. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure. Calibration Executive guides you
 through the required connections.

Note

#### Test
 Limit Equations

The following test limits are derived from the published
 specifications.

| Equation Type | Equation |
| --- | --- |
| Timing Accuracy | 1 year limits are calculated as follows: Limit = Retrace Error + Long Term Stability + Stability vs. Temperature = ±56.5 ppb for one year specs Retrace Error = ±1.5 ppb Long Term Stability = ±50 ppb/year Stability vs. Temperature = ±5 ppb, referenced at 25 °C |

Parent topic:

Calibrating Counter/Timer Devices Using Calibration Executive

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=rename-or-delete-report-configurations.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00197: Rename or Delete Report Configurations

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `rename-or-delete-report-configurations.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/rename-or-delete-report-configurations.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Rename a Report ConfigurationTo rename a report configuration: Select the report you want to rename in the Custom Report Types field. Click Rename. The Rename Report Configuration dialog box opens. Enter a new name in the field, and click Rename. The new name cannot be the same as the previous name,

### Rename or Delete Report Configurations

#### Rename a Report Configuration

To rename a
 report configuration:

1. Select the report you want to rename in the Custom Report
 Types field.
2. Click Rename . The Rename Report Configuration dialog box
 opens.
3. Enter a new name in the field, and click Rename .

The new name cannot be the same as the previous name, and it is subject to the
 same criteria as when you created the report configuration.

#### Delete a Report Configuration

To delete a
 report configuration:

1. Select the report configuration you want to delete in the Custom
 Report Types list.
2. Click Delete .
3. At the confirmation message, click Yes to delete the
 report configuration.

Note

Parent topic:

Custom Report Configurator
