## TOPIC 00225: Calibrating USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 Using Calibration Executive

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `usb-64xx-calibration.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/usb-64xx-calibration.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: Calibrate the USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 using Calibration Executive. Calibration Executive Procedure Features Verify Only Verify & Adjust Manual Mode Optional Adjust Approximate Test Time 131 USB-6421/6451 Test Times Run Options Warmup As-Found Verify Steps Adjust Steps As-Left Verify Steps Total Veri

### Calibrating
 USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 Using Calibration Executive

Calibrate the USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 using Calibration
 Executive.

#### Calibration Executive Procedure
 Features

| Verify Only | Verify & Adjust | Manual Mode | Optional Adjust |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### Approximate Test Time

| Run Options | Warmup | As-Found Verify Steps | Adjust Steps | As-Left Verify Steps | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | 15 minutes | 6 minutes | — | — | 25 minutes |
| Adjust Only | 15 minutes | — | 1 minutes | — | 20 minutes |
| Verify and Adjust | 15 minutes | 6 minutes | 1 minutes | 6 minutes | 30 minutes |

| Run Options | Warmup | As-Found Verify Steps | Adjust Steps | As-Left Verify Steps | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Verify Only | 15 minutes | 12 minutes | — | — | 30 minutes |
| Adjust Only | 15 minutes | — | 1 minutes | — | 20 minutes |
| Verify and Adjust | 15 minutes | 12 minutes | 1 minutes | 12 minutes | 40 minutes |

Note

#### Test Equipment

The following table lists the test equipment required for calibrating USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 modules.

| Instrument | Recommended Model | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Calibrator | Fluke 5730A | — |
| DMM | PXIe-4081 | — |
| Counter | Keysight 53220A | — |
| PC or Controller | — | USB Type C port |
| Type C USB cable | 758787-01 | — |
| Connection adapter required for OEM variant | 789161-01 | — |

Figure 4.

USB-6421/6423/6451/6453

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-DC87E25D-96A5-41D3-AEDE-0B2E9D94C711-a5.png']

Figure 5.

USB-6421/6423/6451/6453

[IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-F8D99774-CB31-4406-AD6A-469BB477848F-a5.png']

#### Test Conditions

The following setup and environmental conditions are required to ensure the USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 meets published
 specifications.

- Keep connections to the device as short as possible. Long cables and wires act
 as antennas, picking up extra noise that can affect measurements.
- Verify that all connections to the DUT are secure.
- Allow adequate warm up time for all components of the calibration system. Warm up time of at
 least 15 minutes is needed to ensure that the USB-6421/6423/6451/6453 measurement circuitry is at a stable operating
 temperature.
- Make all connections as shown in diagrams.
- Use a shielded, twisted pair of copper wires for all cable connections to the
 device to eliminate noise and thermal offsets.
- If a DUT fails reverification after adjustment, ensure that the test conditions
 have been met before returning the DUT to NI.

#### Device Setup

1. Connect the DUT to your controller using the USB C cable.
2. Configure the hardware using Measurement & Automation Explorer (MAX).
3. Launch the Calibration Executive procedure and complete the setup wizard.
 Calibration Executive guides you through the required connections.

#### Setup
 Instructions for OEM Device Variant

1. Using four 4 mm M3 screws, attach four 6 mm M3 standoffs to the corners of the OEM
 board. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-BCED8311-50FF-4DBE-AC18-4AE5CBAA165B-a5.svg']
2. Connect the OEM board to the calibration board and using two 4 mm M3 screws,
 attach two 21 mm M3 standoffs to the top two corners of the calibration
 board. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-CB299454-77B7-4B6A-98F1-9832C14D62CF-a5.svg']
3. Completed assembly. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-3B263E18-B109-43AD-B463-3B4AAF5921E2-a5.svg']

Parent topic:

Calibrating Data Acquisition (DAQ) Devices Using Calibration Executive

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=calibration-executive path=viewing-a-calibration-report.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 00227: Viewing a Calibration Report

- bundle_id: `calibration-executive`
- source_path: `viewing-a-calibration-report.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/calibration-executive/raw/resource/enus/viewing-a-calibration-report.html
- document_id: `calibration-executive`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `concept`
- source_description: You can view calibration reports in one of the following formats: text, HTML, PDF, or Microsoft Word. Select View»Calibration Reports to display the Calibration Reports window, shown in the following figure. Highlight the report you want to view from the displayed list. Use the Report Type field to

### Viewing a Calibration Report

You can view calibration reports in one of the following formats: text, HTML, PDF, or
 Microsoft Word.

1. Select View»Calibration Reports to display the Calibration Reports
 window, shown in the following figure. [IMAGE alt='image' src='GUID-C0F84A6E-0CBF-4180-8AFB-DA04CAAC3BCC-a5.gif']
2. Highlight the report you want to view from the displayed list.
3. Use the Report Type field to select Text, PDF, HTML, or MS Word
 report type.
4. Click View to display the report or click
 Delete to remove the report from the database.
5. Click Close to close the Calibration Reports window and return to the
 main Calibration Executive user interface.

Tip
